alignments.preprint_claim_text: More than 95% of the reported human phosphosites have no known kinases.

alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepKinZero transfers knowledge from kinases with many known target phosphosites to those kinases with no known sites through a zero-shot learning model.
alignments.rationale: This explicit statement about knowledge transfer via zero-shot learning is present in the publication claims but was not separately asserted in the preprint claim list, so it is treated as an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of parents’ and children's neural processes is significantly stronger during maternal demonstrations of positive than negative emotions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Parents and children showed stronger integration of their neural processes during maternal demonstrations of positive than negative emotions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim restates the preprint result with essentially the same wording and strength of inference.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Directed inter-brain metrics indicate that mother-to-infant directional influences are stronger during the expression of positive than negative emotions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Directed inter-brain metrics indicated that mother to infant directional influences were stronger during the expression of positive than negative emotional states. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same directional inter-brain finding with no substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The parent-infant inter-brain network is modulated by the emotional quality and tone of dyadic social interactions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The parent-infant inter-brain network is modulated by the emotional quality and tone of dyadic social interactions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this conclusion verbatim, with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Inter-brain graph metrics may be successfully applied to examine changes in interpersonal network topology. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Inter-brain graph metrics may be successfully applied to examine changes in parent-infant inter-brain network topology. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this methodological contribution, adding an explicit parent-infant context but not changing the claim's substance.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Inter-brain network indices revealed strong effects of emotional valence on the parent-child neural network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This broader summary result appears in the publication but has no distinct counterpart among the listed preprint claims; it aggregates the other findings into a summary statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The strength of the hippocampal-entorhinal dynamical loop is the key parameter governing differential cue control in multi-compartment environments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The strength of the entorhinal-hippocampal dynamical loop is the key parameter governing differential cue control in multi-compartment environments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim matches the preprint claim in meaning and confidence, with only minor lexical reordering of region names.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Construction of spatial representations in visually identical environments requires weak visual cue control. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Construction of separate spatial representations of visually identical compartments required a progressive weakening of visual cue control over place fields in favor of self-motion based mechanisms. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the mechanism (a progressive weakening of visual control toward self-motion) and thus is more specific than the preprint's shorter statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Synaptic plasticity is regulated by the mismatch between visual- and self-motion representations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim asserts the specific regulatory role of mismatch on synaptic plasticity; this specific mechanistic statement is absent in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a functional segregation between plastic and dynamic processes in hippocampal processing. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a functional segregation between plastic and dynamic processes in hippocampal processing. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this conclusion verbatim with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recent innovations in tracking technology have allowed researchers to collect large and highly accurate datasets on animal spatiotemporal position while vastly decreasing the time and cost of collecting such data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent innovations in tracking technology have allowed researchers to collect large and highly accurate datasets on animal spatiotemporal position. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core claim about large, accurate datasets but omits the explicit phrasing about time and cost reductions, narrowing the scope slightly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BEEtag is an open-source, image-based tracking system that allows for unique identification of individual animals or anatomical markers. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BEEtag is an open-source, image-based tracking system that allows for unique identification of individual animals or anatomical markers. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this contribution identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BEEtag independently identifies animals or marked points in each frame of a video, limiting error propagation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BEEtag independently identifies animals or marked points in each frame of a video, limiting error propagation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about per-frame independent identification is preserved unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BEEtag performs well in images with complex backgrounds and is low-cost. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BEEtag performs well in images with complex backgrounds and is low-cost. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the performance and cost claims as stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of BEEtag has been validated by marking and tracking individual bumblebees and recovering individual patterns of space use and activity within the hive. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of BEEtag has been validated by marking and tracking individual bumblebees to recover individual patterns of space use and activity within the nest. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The validation experiment and recovered activity patterns are described equivalently in the publication (nest vs hive terminology change only).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our BRANE Cut approach infers more accurately the five DREAM4 in silico networks with improvements from 6% to 11%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our BRANE Cut approach infers more accurately the five DREAM4 in silico networks with improvements from 6 % to 11 %. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this quantitative benchmarking claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: On a real Escherichia coli compendium, an improvement of 11.8% compared to CLR and 3% compared to GENIE3 is obtained in terms of Area Under Precision-Recall curve. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: On a real Escherichia coli compendium, an improvement of 11.8 % compared to CLR and 3 % compared to GENIE3 is obtained in terms of Area Under Precision-Recall curve. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this quantitative performance claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Up to 48 additional verified interactions are obtained over GENIE3 for a given precision. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Up to 48 additional verified interactions are obtained over GENIE3 for a given precision. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric claim is retained, but the publication reports a lower certainty level, indicating a tempered confidence in the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method achieves a performance similar to that of GENIE3, while being more than seven times faster. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method achieves a performance similar to that of GENIE3, while being more than seven times faster. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance and speed comparison is preserved unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BRANE Cut improves three state-of-the-art GRN inference methods using biologically sound penalties and data-driven parameters. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BRANE Cut improves three state-of-the-art GRN inference methods using biologically sound penalties and data-driven parameters. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim about improving existing methods is carried over without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed mathematical frameworks enable the quantitative assessment of hypoxia gradients within a tumor microenvironment. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed data-informed mechanistic method quantitatively assesses hypoxia in the tumor microenvironment by integrating diverse histopathological data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the assessment claim more specific by describing a particular data-informed mechanistic method and the integration of histopathological data, narrowing the original broader framework claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The modeling and simulations successfully replicate the observed inter- and intra-tumor heterogeneity in hypoxia profiles across various tumor tissues. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework successfully replicates observed inter- and intra-tumor heterogeneity in experimentally obtained hypoxia profiles across various tumor types. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the replication of observed heterogeneity across tumor types/tissues with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A data-driven approach allows for the deduction of partial differential equation models with spatially dependent parameters to understand variability in hypoxia profiles. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a data-driven method to deduce partial differential equation models with spatially dependent parameters, allowing comprehension of the variability of hypoxia profiles within tissues. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same data-driven PDE deduction approach and purpose with equivalent formulation and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework categorizes states of vascularization based on the dynamics of oxygen molecules as identified by the model parameters. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework provides valuable insights from both modeling and biological perspectives, advancing our comprehension of spatio-temporal dynamics of tumor oxygenation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication moves from a specific claim about categorizing vascularization states to a more generalized statement about insights into spatio-temporal oxygen dynamics, softening the original specific categorization claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recent findings suggest that the outer subventricular zone (OSVZ) is decisive for the particular size and complexity of the human cortex. (certainty=low, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The outer subventricular zone (OSVZ) is decisive for the particular size and complexity of the human cortex. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The content is the same claim; the publication increases the stated certainty (low -> medium), so the claim itself is unchanged but confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We establish a multifield computational model that couples cell proliferation and migration at the cell scale with growth and cortical folding at the organ scale. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A multifield computational model has been established to couple cell proliferation and migration with growth and cortical folding. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with equivalent certainty and scope; wording is slightly condensed but meaning is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The presented framework improves our understanding of human brain development. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The presented framework improves understanding of human brain development and could help diagnose and treat neuronal disorders. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication combines the improvement-in-understanding claim with an application (diagnose/treat), thereby clarifying and broadening the original claim while lowering stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model could eventually help diagnose and treat neuronal disorders arising from disruptions in cellular development and associated malformations of cortical development. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The presented framework improves understanding of human brain development and could help diagnose and treat neuronal disorders. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes the same application-oriented concluding statement with similar certainty and scope.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model has been validated based on data from histologically stained sections of the human fetal brain and predicts 3D pattern formation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This validation and specific 3D-prediction result appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The gene drive has a negative effect on the fitness of individuals carrying it, decreasing the total carrying capacity of the population locally in space. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The gene drive has a negative effect on the fitness of individuals carrying it, decreasing the total carrying capacity of the population locally in space. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim and certainty between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The interplay between population dynamics and population genetics can effectively reverse the direction of the invasion and lead to failure. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The interplay between population dynamics and population genetics can effectively reverse the direction of the invasion and lead to failure. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is restated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results of this study can be extended to other bistable systems, such as the spread of cytoplasmic incompatibilities caused by Wolbachia. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results of this study can be extended to other bistable systems, such as the spread of cytoplasmic incompatibilities caused by Wolbachia. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the extension claim verbatim with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Healthy human subjects demonstrated 75% accuracy in estimating belt speeds with a 75 mm/s difference on a two-alternative forced-choice perceptual task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Participants correctly identified a speed difference of 49.7 mm/s in 75% of the trials when walking at 1.05 m/s. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published numerical threshold differs substantially (75 mm/s -> 49.7 mm/s), so the empirical result is revised and indicates a narrower detectable difference; certainty remains high.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The drift-diffusion model effectively accounted for both accuracy and reaction times in the perceptual task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Participants' stimulus perception follows the evidence accumulation process outlined by drift diffusion models. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes that perception follows evidence accumulation (a theoretical conclusion) but does not explicitly restate the model's fit to both accuracy and reaction times, so the claim is revised/narrowed in focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The two-alternative forced-choice tasks can be utilized to investigate subjects' estimates of environmental changes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Individuals can accurately identify smaller speed differences than previously reported. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a specific empirical outcome (better sensitivity) that clarifies and supports the methodological claim about using forced-choice tasks to probe environmental estimates.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach may facilitate the investigation of perceptual deficits and their relation to motor impairments in clinical populations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The clinical-application claim present in the preprint does not appear among the publication's listed claims and so is treated as removed from the published claim set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Readiness Potential (RP) recorded over motor areas has timing consistent with the evidence accumulation process. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: EEG measurement during the task shows that the readiness potential (RP) recorded over motor areas has timing consistent with the evidence accumulation process. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same recorded RP timing finding with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The duration of the RP predicts decision-making time and the duration of evidence accumulation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The duration of the RP predicts decision-making time and the duration of evidence accumulation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical empirical claim and certainty in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RP partly reflects an evidence accumulation process for response selection in the motor system. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The RP partly reflects an evidence accumulation process for response selection in the motor system. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The interpretive conclusion is preserved verbatim with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Evidence accumulation may be a neural implementation of decision-making processes in both perceptual and motor systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Evidence accumulation may be a neural implementation of decision-making processes in both perceptual and motor systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same high-level conclusion about evidence accumulation across systems appears unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The contributions of perceptual categorization and response selection to evidence accumulation processes in decision-making tasks can be potentially evaluated by examining the timing of perceptual and motor EEG signals. (certainty=low, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The contributions of perceptual categorization and response selection to evidence accumulation processes in decision-making tasks can be potentially evaluated by examining the timing of perceptual and motor EEG signals. (certainty=low, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The proposed methodological contribution and its uncertainty are preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identifies key biomarkers governing phenotypic plasticity in pediatric high-grade gliomas, including lineage-plasticity markers and endogenous bioelectric signatures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study predicts lineage-specific plasticity signatures in pediatric high-grade gliomas, including various biomarkers and pathway regulators. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis predicts that specific markers such as KDM5B, ARID5B, and others are drivers of cell fate cybernetics in pHGGs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study predicts lineage-specific plasticity signatures in pediatric high-grade gliomas, including various biomarkers and pathway regulators. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The systems oncology framework developed in this study offers promising avenues for precision medicine by optimizing patient outcomes through targeted therapies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The research combines complex-systems modeling with translational AI to predict plasticity biomarkers and therapeutic targets. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings suggest the epigenetic reprogrammability of tumor phenotypic plasticity and maladaptive behaviors in pHGG ecosystems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Endogenous bioelectric markers have been identified as potential reprogramming targets in pediatric high-grade gliomas. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings suggest that pediatric high-grade glioma cell fates are biased toward neuronal-like identities. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This specific conclusion about neuronal-like fate bias appears in the publication but was not stated in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study supports the robustness of computational predictions by independently recovering previously reported plasticity signatures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit claim about independent recovery/validation of prior signatures that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: "Hard missing" residues often reside in low-confidence regions, but are not exclusively associated with disorder predictions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: "Hard missing" residues often align with low confidence scores. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core result that hard-missing residues align with low-confidence scores but omits the preprint's explicit caveat about not being exclusively associated with disorder predictions, a narrowing/revision of the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis reveals the effectiveness of individual key features in distinguishing between structured and unstructured data. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The incorporation of pLDDT, IUPred scores, and sequence data into the LSTM model has improved the differentiation between structured and unstructured residues. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a more specific, model-oriented statement (features and LSTM) that clarifies and narrows the preprint's more general claim about individual feature effectiveness and presents it with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining key features can provide potential benefits for advanced machine learning applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The incorporation of pLDDT, IUPred scores, and sequence data into the LSTM model has improved the differentiation between structured and unstructured residues. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach aims to uncover varying correlations across different experimental methodologies in the latest structural data. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This research elucidates the relationship between established computational predictions and experimental structural data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint's contribution in slightly different terms—emphasizing elucidation of relationships between computational predictions and experimental data—so the claim is clarified but retains similar scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a bidirectional regulatory relationship between cardiac calcium-regulating proteins and reactive oxygen species, which is integral to the pathogenesis of various cardiac disorders via oxidative stress signaling. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a bidirectional regulatory relationship between cardiac calcium-regulating proteins and reactive oxygen species that is integral to the pathogenesis of various cardiac disorders via oxidative stress signaling. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint claim verbatim in substance and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our machine learning analysis of CaseOLAP scores reveals that the molecular interfaces of Ca 2+ -regulating proteins uniquely associate with cardiac arrhythmias and diseases of the cardiac conduction system. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our machine-learning analysis reveals that the molecular interfaces of calcium-regulating proteins uniquely associate with cardiac arrhythmias and diseases of the cardiac conduction system. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Substantively identical claim and confidence between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A knowledge graph analysis identified 59 of the 128 Ca 2+ -regulating proteins as involved in OS-related cardiac diseases, with cardiomyopathy emerging as the predominant category. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A knowledge graph analysis identified 59 of the 128 calcium-regulating proteins as involved in oxidative stress-related cardiac diseases, with cardiomyopathy emerging as the predominant category. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The insights gained from our study provide a deeper understanding of the molecular interplay between cardiac ROS and Ca 2+ -regulating proteins in the context of cardiovascular diseases. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The insights gained from our study provide a deeper understanding of the molecular interplay between cardiac reactive oxygen species and calcium-regulating proteins in the context of cardiovascular diseases. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The commonly used evaluation strategies for Drug Response Prediction (DRP) methods optimize an unintended DRP score and fail to predict the proper drug-response activity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Commonly used evaluation strategies for Drug Response Prediction methods can be easily fooled by dataset biases. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed new validation paradigm includes three Aggregation Strategies and three train-test Splitting Strategies to ensure a realistic assessment of prediction performance. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new validation protocol with three Aggregation Strategies is proposed to ensure a realistic assessment of prediction performance in DRP methods. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using IC50 as a prediction label is problematic due to its close correlation with drug concentration ranges, which worsens the risk of misleading performance assessment. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using IC50 as a prediction label is problematic due to its close correlation with drug concentration ranges, which can mislead performance assessment. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the limitation about IC50 with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An alternative prediction label for DRP is proposed, which is safer from the perspective of misleading performance assessment. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Area Under the Dose-Response Curve is suggested as a replacement for IC50 to improve prediction assessment. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies a concrete alternative (AUC) whereas the preprint only proposed an alternative label; the publication also expresses this suggestion with lower certainty, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An initial population in East Africa spread and grew from 4000 individuals to 5.7 million in about 132,000 years. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An initial population in East Africa spread and grew from 4000 individuals to 5.7 million in about 132,000 years. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A simple diffusion model from East Africa explains a large portion of the genetic diversity patterns observed in modern humans. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A simple diffusion model from East Africa explains a large portion of the genetic diversity patterns observed in modern humans. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion and certainty as the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Subsequent simulations with demographic estimates produced results nearly identical to those obtained in real data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Subsequent simulations with demographic estimates produced results nearly identical to those obtained in real data. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A model of isolation by distance along the continental landmasses might be the relevant null model to use when investigating selective effects in humans and probably many other species. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A model of isolation by distance along the continental landmasses might be the relevant null model to use when investigating selective effects in humans. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed reservoir model of gated working memory can store an arbitrary real value at random times over an extended period. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The dynamics of the model is a line attractor that learns to exploit reentry and a nonlinearity during the training phase using only a few representative values. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a large range of hyperparameters for which the results of the model hold, indicating that any large enough population of mixed excitatory and inhibitory neurons can learn to realize gated working memory. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings suggest that the property of gated working memory could be an implicit property of any random population that can be acquired through learning. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Evidence integration is key to understanding the neural basis of decision-making. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Evidence integration is key to understanding the neural basis of decision making. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The textual claim is the same but the publication reports a lower stated certainty, so content is unchanged while confidence is reduced.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduced psychophysical task demonstrates evidence integration at long timescales. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduced psychophysical task demonstrates evidence integration at long timescales. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim text and confidence are effectively identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The automated detection of physical objects and boundaries is beneficial for analyzing animal movement relative to their environment. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel Detect+Track approach significantly enhances the accuracy and robustness of animal tracking. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Detect+Track method significantly enhances the accuracy and robustness of animal tracking. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed tools measure an animal’s trajectory, record obstacle position, and detect when the fish passed through virtual gates. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Fish speed, movement direction, and a more precise estimate of body centroid are estimated using the optical flow method. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: An open-source, easy to adopt imaging hardware and trait analysis software method was developed for nematode infection screening. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A cost-effective, 3D-printed imaging device was created to acquire images of the root system of Arabidopsis thaliana infected with Heterodera schachtii. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A cost-effective, 3D-printed imaging device was created to acquire images of the root system of Arabidopsis thaliana infected with Heterodera schachtii. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed imaging device allows for the semi-automated measurement of key traits such as nematode number and size from collected images. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method quantifies root area before infection and accounts for its confounding relationship on nematode infection. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The tools presented in this study have great potential in aiding the understanding of nematode parasitism. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The tools presented in this study have great potential in aiding the understanding of nematode parasitism. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The frontal orienting field (FOF) encodes two separate 2D maps of space: one for planned movements in a self-centered direction reference frame and another for the animal's current position in a world-centered reference frame. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The frontal orienting field (FOF) encodes two separate 2D maps of space: one for planned movements in a self-centered direction reference frame and another for the animal's current position in a world-centered reference frame. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: FOF neurons multiplex both self- and world-reference frame variables at the level of single neurons. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural network model comparison supports the view that the FOF receives target information as self-centered input and generates self-centered planning signals. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Boltzmann reweighting process significantly increases the likelihood of generating promising sex pheromone analogs with improved binding affinities to SfruOR16. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of BEGAN to the design of sex pheromone analogs significantly increases the likelihood of generating promising analogs with improved binding affinities to SfruOR16. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The enhanced method for molecule generation using objective-reinforced GANs allows for exploration of molecular spaces with superior binding properties. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The reweighting process can be refined through multiple iterations without altering the overall distribution shape. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method offers a promising approach for optimizing molecular generation for enhanced protein binding, potentially increasing the efficiency of drug discovery pipelines. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HoloNet is a powerful tool for decoding functional communication events using spatial transcriptomic data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.rationale: The publication does not describe the specific integrative modeling components (ligand–receptor, spatial cell-type distribution, downstream expression) as an explicit claim, indicating this methodological framing was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of HoloNet on Visium datasets revealed the communication landscapes in tumor microenvironments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HoloNet is a powerful tool for illustrating cell-cell communication landscapes and revealing vital FCEs that shape cellular phenotypes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HoloNet uncovered how various ligand–receptor signals and cell types affect specific biological processes. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HoloNet reveals how ligand-receptor signals and cell types affect specific biological processes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this finding directly but with higher stated certainty, so the semantic content is unchanged while confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The stability of HoloNet was validated in a Slideseq-v2 dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HoloNet demonstrates improved reliability in ligand-receptor prioritization compared to existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The dataset-specific stability claim from the preprint is reframed in the publication as a broader comparative reliability improvement; this is a revision and broadening rather than a one-to-one match.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We developed a deep learning model, DeepAnchor, to precisely characterize the binding patterns for different types of CTCF binding sites. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepAnchor accurately characterizes CTCF binding using high-resolution genomic/epigenomic features. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We revealed distinct chromatin and sequence features for CTCF-mediated insulation and looping at a high resolution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Distinct chromatin and sequence patterns for CTCF-mediated insulation and looping have been revealed. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Genomic disruption of CTCF-anchored loops may represent a general causal mechanism of disease pathogenesis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Genomic disruption of CTCF-anchored loops could be a general mechanism of disease pathogenesis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We established a compendium of context-specific CTCF-anchored loops across 52 human tissue/cell types. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: An optimized implementation of a previous loop model based on DeepAnchor score excels in predicting CTCF-anchored loops. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PERSIST reliably identifies gene panels that provide more accurate prediction of the genome-wide expression profile. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PERSIST reliably identifies panels that provide more accurate prediction of the genome-wide expression profile. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PERSIST captures more information with fewer genes in spatial transcriptomics studies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PERSIST captures more information with fewer genes in spatial transcriptomics studies. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PERSIST can be adapted to meet specific biological goals, such as classifying cell types or discerning neuronal electrical properties. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PERSIST can be adapted to specific biological goals. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning algorithms known as protein language models can evolve human antibodies with high efficiency without any information about the target antigen, binding specificity, or protein structure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: General protein language models can efficiently evolve human antibodies by suggesting evolutionarily plausible mutations. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our evolutionary campaigns improved the binding affinities of four clinically relevant antibodies up to 7-fold and three unmatured antibodies up to 160-fold across diverse viral antigens. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm requires only a single wildtype sequence and computes recommended amino acid changes in less than a second. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The same models that improve antibody binding also guide efficient evolution across diverse protein families and selection pressures. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The same models that improve antibody binding also guide efficient evolution across diverse protein families and selection pressures. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Many antibody designs also demonstrate favorable thermostability and viral neutralization activity against Ebola and SARS-CoV-2 pseudoviruses. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate saturated reconstruction of living brain tissue. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An integrated imaging and analysis technology was developed that adapts stimulated emission depletion (STED) microscopy for high SNR and near-isotropic resolution. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The workflow using Prosit for spectral library predictions and the Mistle algorithm is more accurate than a database search using MSFragger. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Anti-CRISPR proteins display diverse inhibition mechanisms through accurate structural prediction and functional analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AMVOC includes an unsupervised deep learning approach that facilitates the discovery and analysis of USV data. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AMVOC includes an unsupervised deep learning approach that facilitates discovery and analysis of USV data. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution of an unsupervised approach and its facilitation of discovery is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AMVOC allows for broader experimental use and the development of previously inaccessible experimental designs for the study of mouse vocal behavior. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AMVOC will facilitate vocal analyses in a broader range of experimental conditions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same concluding benefit but with slightly different phrasing; the scope and certainty remain similar.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The convolutional variational autoencoder (CVAE) learns low dimensional, biophysically relevant latent features from long time-scale protein folding simulations in an unsupervised manner. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The convolutional variational autoencoder (CVAE) learns low dimensional, biophysically relevant latent features from long time-scale protein folding simulations in an unsupervised manner. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Text is effectively identical between preprint and publication with no substantive change in claim, scope, or expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CVAE latent features learned correspond to distinct conformational substates along the protein folding pathways. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CVAE latent features learned correspond to distinct conformational substates along the protein folding pathways. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim matches verbatim and retains the same framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CVAE model predicts nearly 89% of all contacts within the folding trajectories correctly. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CVAE model predicts, on average, nearly 89% of all contacts within the folding trajectories correctly. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication adds the qualifier "on average," slightly clarifying the statistic but preserving the substantive result and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CVAE model can be used to learn latent features of protein folding that can be applied to other independent trajectories. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CVAE model can be used to learn latent features of protein folding that can be applied to other independent trajectories. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this conclusion without substantive change in wording or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CVAE model can quantitatively describe complex biophysical processes such as protein folding. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CVAE model can quantitatively describe complex biophysical processes such as protein folding. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Claim is preserved verbatim with matching confidence and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: COSSMO is able to predict the most frequently used splice site with an accuracy of 70% on unseen test data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: COSSMO is able to predict the most frequently used splice site with an accuracy of 70% on unseen test data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Exact match in wording and reported performance between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: COSSMO achieves an R² of 60% in modeling the PSI distribution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: COSSMO achieves an R2 of 0.6 in modeling the PSI distribution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same numeric result expressed with equivalent notation; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: COSSMO recognizes the consensus splice site sequences as well as many known splicing factors with high specificity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: COSSMO recognizes the consensus splice site sequences and many known splicing factors with high specificity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Content and specificity are equivalent between versions; phrasing differences are cosmetic.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed architecture combining a convolutional neural network with XGBoost significantly enhances prediction accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed architecture combining a convolutional neural network with XGBoost significantly enhances prediction accuracy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The substantive claim is the same; only the manuscript labeling (result vs conclusion) differs, so the claim content and implied applicability remain unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Averaged conductance probability distributions are much more impactful than 2D probability distributions in improving classifier accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Averaged conductance probability distributions are much more impactful than 2D probability distributions in improving classifier accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim is restated identically with the same framing and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The quantitative analysis shows an impressive performance increase of approximately 10% for all sequences analyzed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The quantitative analysis shows an impressive performance increase of approximately 10% for all sequences analyzed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numeric performance improvement is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method developed is generally applicable to other single-molecule identification based on their conductance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method developed is generally applicable to other single-molecule identification based on their conductance. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Content is the same; the publication frames it as a contribution rather than a conclusion but does not alter the claim's substance or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The observed shifts in relative cross coalescent rate curves are unlikely to result from technical artefacts or contributions from an earlier out of Africa population. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A small contribution from the early out of Africa population cannot be ruled out, but it is no longer needed to explain the observed results. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint more strongly ruled out an early out-of-Africa contribution; the publication softens that claim, allowing a small contribution, so the original absolute negation is revised and the certainty reduced.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The shifts in relative cross coalescent rate curves are likely due to a significant bottleneck and slower population growth rate within the Papua New Guinean population. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The observed shifts in Relative Cross Coalescent Rate (RCCR) curves are driven by strong bottleneck and slower population growth rate of the PNG population. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication states the demographic driver more assertively ("are driven by"), increasing expressed strength of attribution relative to the preprint's "likely".

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Papua New Guinean population is positioned as a sister group to other Asian populations, challenging the notion of Papua New Guinean as an outgroup to both European and Asian populations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The PNG population is positioned as a sister group to other East Asian populations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication refines the geographic comparator to East Asian populations (narrowing scope) and expresses the relationship with higher confidence than the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study provides new insights into the complex demographic history of the Papua New Guinean population. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study provides insights on the PNG population and highlights the impact of population-specific demography on interpreting RCCR curves. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication keeps the general contribution but makes explicit the interpretive implication for RCCR curves and increases stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Synaptic connections between engram populations are enhanced in conjunction with synaptogenesis within the hippocampal network after memory formation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: After memory formation, synaptic connections between engram populations are enhanced in conjunction with synaptogenesis within the hippocampal network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim is restated in publication with near-identical wording and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extinction learning significantly correlated with the disappearance of CA3 engram to CA1 engram (E-E) synapses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extinction learning specifically correlates with the disappearance of CA3 engram to CA1 engram synapses. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the correlation but expresses it with somewhat lower certainty/qualification (medium vs high).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A particular distribution pattern of newly formed E-E synapses was observed after memory formation. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No matching statement about a specific distribution pattern of newly formed E-E synapses appears in the publication claims; the claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dynamics at CA3 to CA1 E-E synapses are key sites for modification during fear memory states. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dynamics at CA3 to CA1 engram synapses are key sites for modification during fear memory states. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this conclusion with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Synaptic engrams show elevated synaptic density among engram cells following contextual fear memory formation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific phrasing about elevated synaptic density is present in the publication but not explicitly stated in the preprint claims list, so it is treated as an added claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Larger spine sizes in CA3-CA1 engram synapses correlate with memory strength. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a new, specific result about spine size correlating with memory strength that was not in the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Activity was observed in more than 60% of the selected regions identified as enhancers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Activity was observed in more than 60% of the selected regions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same quantitative observation about activity in >60% of selected regions with essentially identical meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AP-1 alone is responsible for the MES enhancer activity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AP-1 alone is responsible for the MES enhancer activity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the AP-1 responsibility claim verbatim, so no substantive change is present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SOX and MITF both influence MEL enhancer activity, with SOX being required to achieve high levels of activity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates a relationship between long and short sequences in terms of their sequence content, enhancer activity, and specificity across melanoma cell states. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our MPRAs shed light on the relationship between long and short sequences in terms of their sequence content, enhancer activity, and specificity across melanoma cell states. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Task-Assisted Generative Adversarial Network (TA-GAN) improves generative accuracy over unassisted methods for predicting super-resolved microscopy images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The task-assisted generative adversarial network (TA-GAN) improves generative accuracy over unassisted methods. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TA-GAN allows for the identification of dividing S. aureus bacterial cell boundaries and localization of nanodomains in simulated images of dendritic spines. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly report these specific application examples (S. aureus boundaries, dendritic spine nanodomains), so the concrete preprint examples are not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TA-GAN can be incorporated directly into the microscope's acquisition pipeline to inform users about the nanometric content of the field of view without needing super-resolved images. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The TA-GAN can predict the nanometric content of the field of view without requiring the acquisition of a super-resolved image. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of TA-GAN optimizes the acquisition sequence and reduces light exposure during microscopy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model generates accurate images of aggregates and streams, allowing for the estimation of aggregate positions and sizes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model generates accurate images of aggregates and streams, allowing for the estimation of aggregate positions and sizes. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Further training on ripple patterns enables accurate estimation of the rippling wavelength. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methods developed are applicable for many other phenotypic behaviors and pattern formation studies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methods developed are applicable for many other phenotypic behaviors and pattern formation studies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both versions present the same generalisability claim without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GAT-GO greatly outperforms the latest sequence- and structure-based deep learning methods in protein function prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GAT-GO greatly outperforms the latest sequence- and structure-based deep learning methods in protein function prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: GAT-GO yields AUPRC scores of 0.427, 0.253, and 0.411 for the MFO, BPO, and CCO ontology domains on the PDB-mmseqs testset, significantly better than the homology-based method BLAST. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: On the PDB-cdhit testset, GAT-GO obtains Fmax scores of 0.637, 0.501, and 0.542 for the MFO, BPO, and CCO ontology domains, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: On the PDB-cdhit testset, GAT-GO obtains Fmax scores of 0.637, 0.501, and 0.542 for the MFO, BPO, and CCO ontology domains, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepMAsED accurately identifies misassemblies in metagenome-assembled contigs from a broad diversity of bacteria and archaea without the need for reference genomes or strong modelling assumptions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepMAsED accurately identifies misassemblies in metagenome-assembled contigs from a broad diversity of bacteria and archaea without the need for reference genomes or strong modeling assumptions. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepMAsED accuracy substantially exceeds the state-of-the-art when applied to large and complex metagenome assemblies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepMAsED accuracy substantially exceeds the state-of-the-art when applied to large and complex metagenome assemblies. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model estimates close to a 5% contig misassembly rate in two recent large-scale metagenome assembly publications. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepMAsED is a flexible misassembly classifier that can be applied to a wide range of metagenome assembly projects. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Smaller medial-lateral distance between approaching pedestrians and a lower number of surrounding pedestrians predicted an increase in the likelihood of a subsequent path deviation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Smaller medial-lateral distance between approaching pedestrians and smaller crowd size predicted an increased likelihood of a subsequent path deviation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Whether a pedestrian looked distracted or held, pushed, or pulled something while walking predicted the medial-lateral distance between pedestrians at the time of crossing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Whether a pedestrian looked distracted or held, pushed, or pulled an object predicted medial-lateral distance between pedestrians at the time of crossing.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same predictive relationship with only minor wording changes, leaving content and confidence unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pedestrians maintained a larger personal space boundary compared to lab settings, likely due to the outdoor path’s width.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results highlight both similarities and differences with lab-based behaviour.
alignments.rationale: The preprint made a specific claim about larger personal space and a likely cause; the publication generalizes to both similarities and differences with lab behaviour, reducing the specific causal assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Collision avoidance behaviours in lab and real-world environments share similarities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results highlight both similarities and differences with lab-based behaviour.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings offer insights relevant to developing more accurate computational models for realistic pedestrian movement.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings offer insights relevant to developing accurate computational models for realistic pedestrian movement.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same contribution about informing computational models with only slight wording variation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BowSaw can accurately recover the sets of variables used to generate phenotypes through complex Boolean rules, even under challenging noise levels.
alignments.pub_claim_text: BowSaw can accurately recover the sets of variables used to generate phenotypes through complex Boolean rules, even under challenging noise levels.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the simulation performance claim verbatim, with no substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of BowSaw to data from the integrative Human Microbiome Project reveals previously unreported high-order combinations of microbial taxa putatively associated with Crohn’s disease.
alignments.pub_claim_text: BowSaw identifies previously unreported high-order combinations of microbial taxa putatively associated with Crohn's disease.
alignments.rationale: Content is the same but the publication down-weights certainty (high -> medium), so the semantic content is unchanged but confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BowSaw provides a new way of using decision trees to generate testable biological hypotheses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: BowSaw provides a new way of using decision trees to generate testable biological hypotheses.
alignments.rationale: The claim is unchanged in substance, but the publication increases the emphasis/positioning (medium -> high/conclusion), indicating amplified confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PWAS is a new method for detecting protein-coding genes associated with phenotypes through protein function alterations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PWAS is a new method for detecting gene-phenotype associations mediated by protein function alterations.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same methodological contribution with slightly different wording (protein-coding genes vs gene-phenotype associations) but no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PWAS can detect subtle patterns that standard GWAS and other methods overlook.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A comparison with GWAS and other existing methods proves PWAS's capacity to recover causal protein-coding genes and highlight new associations.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the ability from detecting subtle patterns to claiming proven capacity to recover causal genes and new associations, a stronger and more specific claim (hence revised and amplified).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PWAS captures more complex modes of heritability, including recessive inheritance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PWAS can capture complex modes of heritability, including recessive inheritance.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about capturing complex heritability modes, including recessive inheritance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The discovered associations by PWAS are supported by a concrete molecular model, thus reducing the gap to inferring causality.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A comparison with GWAS and other existing methods proves PWAS's capacity to recover causal protein-coding genes and highlight new associations.
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasized support via a molecular model and reduced gap to causality; the publication focuses on comparative evidence that PWAS recovers causal genes, shifting emphasis and wording (thus revised).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PWAS has been applied on a cohort derived from the UK Biobank and evaluated on 49 prominent phenotypes.
alignments.rationale: The publication claims list does not include the dataset/application detail present in the preprint, indicating this specific application claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The immuneSIM R package allows the simulation of native-like and aberrant synthetic full length variable region immune receptor sequences.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The immuneSIM R package allows the simulation of native-like and aberrant synthetic full-length variable region immune receptor sequences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ImmuneSIM enables the tuning of various immune receptor features including species, chain type, germline gene usage, and clonal abundance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The simulation in immuneSIM can be tuned by various immune receptor features such as species, chain type, and germline gene usage.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Each simulated sequence in immuneSIM is annotated by the complete set of simulation events that contributed to its in silico generation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Each simulated sequence in immuneSIM is annotated by the complete set of simulation events that contributed to its in silico generation.
alignments.rationale: This result is restated verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ImmuneSIM permits the benchmarking of key computational tools for immune receptor analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: immuneSIM enables the benchmarking of key computational tools for immune receptor analysis.
alignments.rationale: The publication elevates this point to a contribution with higher certainty (medium -> high), so the claim content is the same but confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method using k-motifs achieves up to 94% accuracy in classifying repetitive behavior versus controls.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method using k-motifs achieves up to 94% accuracy in classifying repetitive behaviour versus controls.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the numeric accuracy result identically (minor spelling variation only).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy of the k-motifs classifier represents a substantial improvement over existing methods, which achieve up to 88% accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new approach to quantify behaviour in an unconstrained environment improves classification accuracy compared to existing methods, which achieve up to 88% accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Visualization of movement and time patterns is highly predictive of behaviors in the open field.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Visualisation of movement/time patterns is highly predictive of behaviours in the context of the study.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning approach allows for the creation of features in a data-driven fashion applicable to various behavioral analyses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The k-motifs method can be applied to behavioural analysis across various experimental paradigms using machine learning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The assembly of the bacterial-sized mitogenome of Norway spruce was achieved using machine learning and in silico enrichment of mitochondrial-like long reads. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The assembly of the bacterial-sized mitogenome of Norway spruce was achieved using machine learning and in silico enrichment of mitochondrial-like long reads. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Text is effectively identical between preprint and publication, no substantive change in content, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recombination dynamics in plant mitogenomes were found to be heterogeneous across vascular plants. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recombination dynamics in plant mitogenomes were found to be heterogeneous across vascular plants. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Highly active small repeats (approximately 200 bp) were present in about a third of the studied mitogenomes. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Variation in recombination rates is identified as an underinvestigated source of plant mitogenome diversity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study identified variation in recombination rates as an underinvestigated source of plant mitogenome diversity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We report a novel FRET-based assay named the Free Ub Sensor System (FUSS) to study the in vitro kinetics of ubiquitination. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We report a novel Förster resonance energy transfer (FRET)-based assay for studying the in vitro kinetics of ubiquitination, named the free Ub sensor system (FUSS). (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Ubiquitination results in decreasing FRET efficiency, from which reaction rates can be determined. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Ubiquitination results in decreasing FRET efficiency, from which reaction rates can be determined. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Increasing the concentration of CHIP or UbcH5 enhances ubiquitination rates, while Uba1 does not have this effect. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Increasing the concentration of CHIP or UbcH5 but not Uba1 enhances ubiquitination rates. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The geneEXPLORER model quantifies the collective effects of both cis- and trans-methylations on gene expression. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Data Portal provides a secure, fully auditable, remote access environment for the analysis of cohort data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Over the last 2 years, 160 project proposals involving 579 individual cohort data access requests were received from 268 applicants across 72 institutions in 16 countries. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We developed a framework for using assigned chemical shift data to guide RNA secondary structure prediction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We developed a framework for using assigned chemical shift data to guide RNA secondary structure prediction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results showed that we could recover the correct secondary folds for nearly all of the 108 RNAs in our dataset with remarkable accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our results showed that we could recover the correct secondary fold of most of the 108 RNAs in our data set with remarkable accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We found that by using folding restraints derived from chemical shifts, we could recover the two distinct structures of the miR-20b. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We confirmed our ability to conditionally predict the secondary structure of the model RNA, microRNA-20b (miR-20b). (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We found that by using the base-pairing status predictions obtained from assigned chemical shift data as folding restraints, we could generate structures that closely resembled the known structure of the two distinct states. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: A command-line tool for chemical shifts to base-pairing status predictions in RNA has been incorporated into our CS2Structure Git repository. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a concrete software/tool release claim (command-line tool in repository) that does not appear as an explicit claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The presented algorithm can accurately identify first through fourth-degree relationships in an endogamous population.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm accurately identifies first through fourth-degree relationships in an endogamous population.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel 'haplotype score' can distinguish half-sibling pairs from avuncular or grandparent-grandchildren pairs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel 'haplotype score' can distinguish half-sibling pairs from avuncular or grandparent-grandchildren pairs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm demonstrates effectiveness in a sample of 700 individuals from the Himba population, which has a high proportion of half-sibships.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm demonstrates effectiveness in both the Himba population and in datasets from the Barbados Asthma Genetics Study and a European-descent population from Quebec.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Accurate reconstruction of relatives facilitates estimation of allele frequencies and other population genomic questions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Single-cell RNA-seq can yield high-resolution cell-type-specific expression signatures that reveal new cell types and the developmental trajectories of cell lineages.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Single cell RNA sequencing can yield high-resolution cell-type-specific expression signatures that reveal new cell types and the developmental trajectories of cell lineages.
