alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of fungal and oomycete effectors in training leads to a higher number of predicted cytoplasmic effectors in biotrophic fungi. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of fungal and oomycete effectors in training leads to a higher number of predicted cytoplasmic effectors in biotrophic fungi. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EffectorP 3.0 expands predicted effector repertoires beyond small, cysteine-rich secreted proteins in fungi and RxLR-motif containing secreted proteins in oomycetes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: EffectorP 3.0 expands predicted effector repertoires beyond small, cysteine-rich secreted proteins in fungi and RxLR-motif containing secreted proteins in oomycetes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Signal peptide prediction is essential for accurate effector prediction, as EffectorP 3.0 recognizes a cytoplasmic signal also in intracellular, non-secreted proteins. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Signal peptide prediction is essential for accurate effector prediction, because EffectorP 3.0 recognizes a cytoplasmic signal also in intracellular, nonsecreted proteins. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel dataset was introduced to train and test machine learning models, addressing the overlapping data issue in the positive training and testing datasets. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MDFP has the potential to be an alternative to traditional molecular fingerprints for predicting hERG cardiotoxicity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: CHESS 3 represents an improved human gene catalog based on nearly 10,000 RNA-seq experiments across 54 body sites. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sugar alcohols, carbocyclic sugar, and vitamins may contribute to the growth of Faecalibacterium prausnitzii. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Future nutritional studies should explore a personalized approach accounting for strain-level genetic variations and community-level microbiome composition to increase the relative abundance of Faecalibacterium prausnitzii. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Future nutritional studies should explore a personalized approach to increase the relative abundance of Faecalibacterium prausnitzii, accounting for strain-level genetic variations and community-level microbiome composition. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Robust and strain-dependent growth patterns of Faecalibacterium prausnitzii were observed on sorbitol and inositol. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Fecal communities that increased Faecalibacterium prausnitzii on inulin also showed at least 60% more Faecalibacterium prausnitzii on inositol containing media than control. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: cDNA display proteolysis is a new method for measuring thermodynamic folding stability for up to 900,000 protein domains in a one-week experiment. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The cDNA display proteolysis method can measure thermodynamic folding stability for up to 900,000 protein domains in a one-week experiment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same methodological throughput and timeframe with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset curated from 1.8 million measurements includes ~850,000 high-quality folding stabilities covering all single amino acid variants and selected double mutants of 354 natural and 188 de novo designed protein domains. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset curated from 1.8 million measurements includes around 776,000 high-quality folding stabilities covering all single amino acid variants and selected double mutants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a lower count of high-quality stability measurements than the preprint, a numeric revision while preserving the overall dataset description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study quantified environmental factors influencing amino acid fitness, thermodynamic couplings between protein sites, and the global divergence between evolutionary amino acid usage and protein folding stability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study quantified environmental factors influencing amino acid fitness, thermodynamic couplings between protein sites, and the divergence between evolutionary amino acid usage and protein folding stability. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same set of quantified analyses but reports them with lower stated certainty (medium vs high), thus tempering confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The cDNA display proteolysis method is fast, accurate, and uniquely scalable, promising to reveal the quantitative rules for how amino acid sequences encode folding stability. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The cDNA display proteolysis method is fast, accurate, and uniquely scalable, promising to reveal the quantitative rules for how amino acid sequences encode folding stability. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the certainty of this methodological conclusion (medium -> high) while keeping the conclusion itself unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Loss of the tumour suppressor PTEN in high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma is associated with a patient subgroup with poor prognosis.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim explicitly states that PTEN loss defines a poor-prognosis HGSOC subgroup; related outcome claims focus on ARF6-centred complex expression rather than PTEN status, so the preprint claim appears removed in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PTEN deficiency induces PI(3,4,5)P3-rich and -dependent membrane protrusions into the extracellular matrix, resulting in a collective invasion phenotype.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PTEN deficiency induces PI(3,4,5)P3-rich and -dependent membrane protrusions into the extracellular matrix, resulting in a collective invasion phenotype.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim with the same mechanistic and phenotypic wording, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The small GTPase ARF6 is identified as a crucial vulnerability upon PTEN loss.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The small GTPase ARF6 is identified as a crucial vulnerability of HGSOC cells upon PTEN loss.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same finding about ARF6 as a vulnerability in PTEN-deficient HGSOC cells with equivalent strength and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The expression of the ARF6-centred complex in HGSOC patients is inversely associated with outcome.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The expression of the CYTH2-ARF6-AGAP1 complex in HGSOC patients is inversely associated with outcome.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ARF6 may represent a new therapeutic vulnerability in PTEN-depleted HGSOC tumours.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ARF6 may represent a therapeutic vulnerability in PTEN-depleted HGSOC.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Distinct and contrasting regimes of molecular evolution across exons, introns, untranslated regions, and mitochondrial genomes have been identified in living birds.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Distinct macroevolutionary regimes were identified across exons, introns, untranslated regions, and mitochondrial genomes in avian molecular evolution.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this core result using equivalent terms (macroevolutionary regimes/avain molecular evolution) with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Up to fifteen shifts in the mode of avian molecular evolution map to rapidly diversifying clades near the Cretaceous-Palaeogene boundary, demonstrating a burst of genomic disparity early in the evolutionary history of crown birds.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Bird clades originating near the K-Pg boundary exhibited numerous shifts in the mode of molecular evolution.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same observation about numerous shifts near the K-Pg boundary but uses slightly less numeric specificity; the core claim is preserved and clarified in phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Shifts in developmental mode or adult body mass best explain transitions in the mode of nucleotide substitution in birds.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Inferred shifts in substitution patterns were closely related to evolutionary shifts in developmental mode, adult body mass, and patterns of metabolic scaling.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's stronger causal phrasing ('best explain') is softened in the publication to 'closely related,' reducing causal strength and tempering confidence while retaining the same associated factors.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The scaling relationship between basal metabolic rate and body mass appears to have weakened across the end-Cretaceous transition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Inferred shifts in substitution patterns were closely related to evolutionary shifts in developmental mode, adult body mass, and patterns of metabolic scaling.
alignments.rationale: The publication links metabolic scaling patterns to evolutionary shifts but does not explicitly state that the BMR–body mass scaling relationship 'weakened,' so the original directional claim is weakened/modified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Chicxulub bolide impact triggered integrated patterns of evolution across avian genomes, physiology, and life history that structured the evolutionary potential of modern birds.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The end-Cretaceous mass extinction triggered integrated patterns of evolution across avian genomes, physiology, and life history.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the driver from the specific Chicxulub bolide to the broader end-Cretaceous mass extinction; this broadens the causal framing and lowers expressed certainty (medium vs high).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Phenotypic heterogeneity in Escherichia coli cells under hydrogen peroxide stress has a deterministic origin.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Phenotypic heterogeneity in Escherichia coli under hydrogen peroxide stress arises from a precise and rapid feedback between each cell and its immediate environment.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the mechanistic basis ('precise and rapid feedback between each cell and its immediate environment'), clarifying the deterministic origin described in the preprint without changing scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The spatio-temporal dynamics in hydrogen peroxide concentration are influenced by the scavenging capacity and morphology of cells in the local environment.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cell-cell interaction plays a crucial role in phenotypic heterogeneity, as cells shield each other from hydrogen peroxide via their individual stress responses.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the same mechanism in terms of cell–cell interactions and shielding via individual responses, which clarifies and emphasizes the social aspect of the scavenging/morphology-mediated dynamics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Single cells optimize their individual responses to oxidative stress, which contributes to increased stress tolerance at the population level.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The collective phenotype resulting from short-range cell-cell interactions protects a large proportion of the bacterial population.
alignments.rationale: The publication describes the emergent collective protection arising from short-range interactions, which clarifies how individual optimization scales to population-level tolerance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The scavenging activities of individual cells lead to spatial variation in mutation rates within the population.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or discuss spatial variation in mutation rates resulting from scavenging, so this specific downstream claim is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Similarity-based merger models outperformed other models with an additional 20% assays achieving AUC>0.70.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Similarity-based merger models outperformed other models with an additional 20% assays achieving an AUC > 0.70.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this quantitative performance result verbatim, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The similarity-based merger models combined structure and cell morphology models to more accurately predict biological assay outcomes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results demonstrated that combining structure and cell morphology models can more accurately predict biological assay outcomes.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same conclusion that combining structure and morphology models improves prediction accuracy with the same strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The applicability domain of the models was expanded by better extrapolating to new structural and morphology spaces.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The applicability domain of the models was expanded by better extrapolating to new structural and morphology spaces.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this claim about expanded applicability domain with identical meaning and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bending forces evoke structural transitions in F-actin which are modulated by actin nucleotide state.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The nucleotide state of actin modulates F-actin structural transitions evoked by bending forces.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this core finding in equivalent terms, preserving meaning and strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cryo-electron microscopy structures of ADP- and ADP-P i -F-actin reveal inter-subunit interactions primarily bridged by waters which could contribute to lattice flexibility.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cryo-electron microscopy structures reveal intersubunit interfaces bridged by water molecules that could mediate filament lattice flexibility.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same structural observation about water-bridged intersubunit interfaces and their potential role in lattice flexibility.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Phosphate rigidifies actin subunits to alter F-actin’s bending structural landscape.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Phosphate rigidifies actin subunits to alter the bending structural landscape of F-actin.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this mechanistic conclusion about phosphate-induced rigidification with equivalent wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Actin nucleotide state serves as a co-regulator of F-actin mechanical regulation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Actin nucleotide state can serve as a co-regulator of F-actin mechanical regulation.
alignments.rationale: The publication softens phrasing slightly ('can serve') but retains the same conceptual claim that nucleotide state contributes to mechanical regulation; this is a minor clarification in tone.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bending forces evoke nucleotide-state dependent conformational transitions that could be readily detected by actin binding proteins.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the specific claim that these nucleotide-state dependent transitions would be readily detectable by actin-binding proteins, so this particular inference is absent in the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Randomized Singular Value Decomposition (RSVD) algorithm implemented in PCAone achieves significantly faster computation time while maintaining accuracy comparable to the slower Implicitly Restarted Arnoldi Method (IRAM). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed PCAone algorithm achieves significantly faster computation time while maintaining accuracy comparable to the slower Implicitly Restarted Arnoldi Method (IRAM). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same performance vs IRAM claim with equivalent emphasis and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PCAone accurately computes the top 40 principal components of the UK Biobank dataset, comprising around 0.5 million individuals and 6.1 million common SNPs, within 9 hours. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PCAone accurately computes the top 40 principal components of the UK Biobank data within 9 hours, utilizing less than 20 GB of memory and 20 CPU threads. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the UK Biobank 9-hour result but adds resource-usage details (memory/threads), clarifying computational requirements.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When applied to single-cell RNA sequencing data featuring 1.3 million cells, PCAone captures the top 40 principal components in 49 minutes, representing a 10-fold improvement over state-of-the-art tools. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: When applied to single-cell RNA sequencing data, PCAone captures the top 40 principal components in 49 minutes, representing a 10-fold improvement over state-of-the-art tools. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same single-cell runtime and improvement claim without meaningful change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PCAone utilizes less than 20 GB of memory and 20 CPU threads to effectively capture population structure, signals of selection, structural variants, and low recombination regions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PCAone accurately computes the top 40 principal components of the UK Biobank data within 9 hours, utilizing less than 20 GB of memory and 20 CPU threads. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the resource-usage element but omits the preprint's broader list of biological signals, narrowing the claim to compute-time/resource usage for UKB.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The molten globule (MG) state of some proteins is functionally relevant despite lacking a well-defined tertiary structure. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The molten globule (MG) state of some proteins is functionally relevant despite lacking a well-defined tertiary structure. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion about functional relevance of the MG state with equivalent phrasing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MG state of proteins exhibits dynamic conformational fluctuations on nanosecond time scales. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The MG state of a protein is dynamic, with conformational states interconverting on nanosecond time scales. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the dynamics-on-nanosecond-timescales result with only minor wording changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Residues responsible for metastability in the MG state prefer unstructured or bend conformations, contrasting with their stable helical structure in the crystal form. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Residues responsible for metastability in the MG state prefer unstructured or bent conformations, contrasting with their stable helical structure in the crystal form. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same residue-conformation result; wording differences are trivial.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MG state of protein behaves similarly to intrinsic disordered proteins, with disorder induced by external conditions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The MG state of a protein behaves similarly to intrinsic disordered proteins, with disorder induced by external conditions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conceptual conclusion about MG similarity to intrinsically disordered proteins.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: βAR stimulation significantly altered nearly all AP waveform parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: βAR stimulation significantly altered nearly all AP waveform parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same broad effect of βAR stimulation on AP waveform parameters.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The increase in AP firing rate was only correlated with changes in a subset of parameters: diastolic duration, late AP duration, and diastolic depolarization rate. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The increase in firing rate was only correlated with changes in a subset of parameters (diastolic duration, late AP duration, and diastolic depolarization rate). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same correlation between firing rate increase and a subset of waveform parameters.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dynamic clamp injection of the βAR-sensitive component of I f accounts for approximately 41% of the fight-or-flight increase in AP firing rate. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dynamic clamp injection of the βAR-sensitive component of If accounts for ∼41% of the fight-or-flight increase in AP firing rate. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the dynamic-clamp quantitative contribution (~41%) without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: I f accounts for 60% of the decrease in the interval between APs during the fight-or-flight response. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: If accounts for 60% of the decrease in the interval between APs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same quantitative attribution (60%) to If for interval decrease.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: I f is an essential contributor to the fight-or-flight increase in heart rate. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: If is an essential contributor to the fight-or-flight increase in heart rate. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion about If being an essential contributor.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: scAEGAN outperforms Seurat3 in library integration. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The scAEGAN outperforms Seurat3 in library integration. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same comparative performance claim against Seurat3.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: scAEGAN is more robust against data sparsity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: scAEGAN is more robust against data sparsity compared to existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly compares robustness to 'existing methods', clarifying the comparative context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: scAEGAN beats Seurat 4 in integrating paired data from the same cell. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: scAEGAN beats Seurat 4 in integrating paired data from the same cell. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result versus Seurat4 with consistent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In predicting one data modality from another, scAEGAN outperforms Babel. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In predicting one data modality from another, scAEGAN outperforms Babel. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the predictive-modality comparison to Babel unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: scAEGAN surpasses current state-of-the-art methods across several seemingly different integration challenges. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: scAEGAN surpasses current state-of-the-art methods and unifies integration and prediction challenges. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication extends the conclusion by adding that scAEGAN 'unifies' integration and prediction, broadening the claim beyond mere superiority.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Zebrafish exploratory behavior can be categorized into four distinct behavioral subtypes: bold, shy, wall-huggers, and active explorers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Zebrafish exploratory behavior stratifies into four distinct clusters: bold, shy, wall-huggers, and active explorers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same four-cluster behavioral stratification with equivalent specificity and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The identified behavioral subtypes in zebrafish are stable for up to 10 weeks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The behavioral clusters identified in zebrafish are stable across time and influenced by strain and sex. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the preprint's 'stable for up to 10 weeks' to 'stable across time' and merges it with strain/sex effects, broadening the temporal claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The behavioral subtypes of zebrafish are influenced by genetic strain and sex. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The behavioral clusters identified in zebrafish are stable across time and influenced by strain and sex. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication integrates the strain-and-sex influence into a primary result with higher certainty, amplifying confidence compared to the preprint conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individual differences in zebrafish exploratory behavior exhibit greater complexity than the traditional bold versus shy dichotomy suggests. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings suggest that zebrafish exploratory behavior has greater complexity than previously recognized. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same conclusion about increased behavioral complexity using similar phrasing and certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The research lays the groundwork for using zebrafish to understand the biological basis of individual differences in behavior. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This contribution--positioning the work as groundwork for broader biological investigations--is not stated in the preprint claims and appears as a new added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The unpollinated carpel undergoes a well-defined initial growth phase, followed by a peak phase and a final deterioration phase. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The unpollinated carpel undergoes a well-defined initial growth phase, followed by a peak phase and a final deterioration phase. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim text is essentially identical between preprint and publication, so meaning and certainty are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developmental dynamics of the unpollinated carpel were largely consistent across years. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report a statement about cross-year consistency of developmental dynamics, so this preprint claim is not reflected.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The phenotyping approach developed in this study is suitable for the detailed study of floral organ traits in field grown plants. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate the suitability of a high-throughput phenotyping approach for the analysis of floral organ traits in field-grown plants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication restates the same suitability of the phenotyping approach for field-grown floral organ trait analysis with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The absolute duration of each developmental phase of the unpollinated carpel varied across years. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report variability in absolute phase durations across years, so this specific result is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This new phenotyping approach may help advance research into carpel morphology and female fertility in wheat. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This phenotyping approach provides a new tool for examining carpel development, which may advance research into female fertility of wheat. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication expresses the same potential contribution but frames it as a new tool for examining carpel development and states it with higher certainty, slightly clarifying and amplifying the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: spongEffects infers subnetworks from ceRNA networks and calculates patient-specific scores related to their regulatory activity. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state the internal inference of subnetworks or the exact calculation of patient-specific regulatory scores as an explicit claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: spongEffects can be used for tumor classification and identifying subtype-specific regulatory interactions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: spongEffects can be used for downstream interpretation and machine learning tasks such as tumor classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the tumor-classification application but omits the explicit claim about identifying subtype-specific regulatory interactions, narrowing the original preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: spongEffects prioritizes ceRNA modules as biomarkers and provides insights into the miRNA regulatory landscape. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: spongEffects prioritizes ceRNA modules as biomarkers and offers insights into the miRNA regulatory landscape. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this claim verbatim in both content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Module scores from spongEffects can be inferred from gene expression data alone, allowing application to cohorts lacking miRNA expression information. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Module scores from spongEffects can be inferred from gene expression data alone, allowing application to cohorts lacking miRNA expression information. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same statement about inferring module scores from gene expression alone with similar certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Maternal immune activation combined with adolescent alcohol exposure leads to increased alcohol consumption in adult offspring. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dual rats, particularly females, drank significantly more alcohol in adulthood compared to all other groups. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the increased adult alcohol consumption and adds a sex-specific detail (particularly females), which clarifies the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dual rats, particularly females, drank significantly more alcohol in adulthood compared to all other groups. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dual rats, particularly females, drank significantly more alcohol in adulthood compared to all other groups. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This claim appears verbatim in the publication with the same specificity and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning models can differentiate Dual rats from control and AE rats in both sexes, and from MIA rats in females. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using machine learning to build predictive models from LFPs, we were able to differentiate Dual rats from control rats and AE rats in both sexes, and Dual rats from MIA rats in females. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports essentially the same machine-learning differentiation result with comparable detail and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MIA combined with AE is a valuable model for studying the neurobiological mechanisms of co-occurring disorders. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This model may be valuable for studying the neurobiological underpinnings of co-occurring disorders. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both versions express the same conclusion about the model's value for studying neurobiological underpinnings of co-occurring disorders with similar caution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The microstructure of major white matter tracts selectively predicted learning outcomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Tract microstructure selectively predicted learning outcomes, with left hemisphere pArc and SLF3 tracts predicting drawing learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies which tracts and which tasks; it refines the general preprint claim by adding tract- and task-level detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Left hemisphere pArc and SLF 3 tracts predicted drawing learning, while the left hemisphere MDLFspl predicted visual recognition learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Tract microstructure selectively predicted learning outcomes, with left hemisphere pArc and SLF3 tracts predicting drawing learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly reports the pArc and SLF3-drawing link (exact match) and separately reports MDLFspl for visual recognition (see Q1); this preprint claim maps best to the paper's detailed findings though it spans two published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individual differences in the microstructure of human white matter tracts may be selectively related to future learning outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Individual differences in the microstructure of human white matter tracts may be selectively related to future learning outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this concluding statement with the same caveated wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prediction model for learning a sensorimotor task did not transfer to other learning outcomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's set of claims does not mention lack of transfer of a sensorimotor prediction model, so this specific result is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A selective mapping between tract microstructure and future learning has been demonstrated in humans. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Individual differences in the microstructure of human white matter tracts may be selectively related to future learning outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same general conclusion about selective mapping between tract microstructure and future learning with similar caution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AnglesRefine method generally surpasses other techniques on the CASP11-14 test dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental outcomes indicate that our method generally surpasses other techniques on the CASP11-14 test dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this result directly, reporting superior performance on CASP11-14 with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AnglesRefine performs comparably or marginally better on the CASP15 test dataset compared to other methods. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report results or comparisons specifically for CASP15, so this preprint claim is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method consistently demonstrates the least likelihood of model quality degradation, with a degradation percentage of less than 10%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method consistently demonstrates the least likelihood of model quality degradation, with a degradation percentage of less than 10%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication states this result verbatim with the same metric and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AnglesRefine significantly reduces computational time compared to existing protein structure refinement methods. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach significantly reduces computational time compared to existing refinement methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the computational-time reduction claim but reports it with lower (medium) certainty than the preprint's high certainty, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Metabolic reprogramming plays a crucial role in the long-term consequences of COVID-19 infection.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim explicitly asserts that metabolic reprogramming plays a crucial role in long-term COVID-19 consequences; the published claims focus on methods, subgroups, and specific signatures rather than this causal conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of machine learning and SHAP values allows for the uncovering of metabolomic signatures and potential biomarkers for COVID-19 and Post-COVID-19 conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study highlights the potential of integrating machine learning and explainable artificial intelligence for a detailed understanding of metabolic anomalies in COVID-19 and Post-COVID-19 conditions.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same idea but uses more cautious language ('highlights the potential') and frames it as enabling understanding rather than asserting definitive biomarker discovery.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: XGBoost models enhanced by SHAP for explainability demonstrate superior predictive performance compared to traditional analysis methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: XGBoost, combined with SHAP, outperformed traditional methods in predicting metabolic alterations in COVID-19 and Post-COVID-19 patients.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result that XGBoost+SHAP outperformed traditional methods in prediction, matching the preprint claim closely.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis revealed several metabolomic subgroups within the COVID-19 and Post-COVID-19 conditions, indicating heterogeneous metabolic responses to the infection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis revealed metabolomic subgroups within the COVID-19 and Post-COVID-19 conditions, indicating heterogeneous metabolic responses to the infection.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this finding verbatim, indicating no substantive change in meaning or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CpG Transformer displays state-of-the-art performances on a wide range of scBS-seq and scRRBS-seq datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CpG Transformer displays state-of-the-art performances on a wide range of scBS-seq and scRRBS-seq datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the performance claim directly and with equivalent confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CpG Transformer demonstrates interpretability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CpG Transformer demonstrates interpretability and rapid transfer learning properties.
alignments.rationale: The publication combines interpretability with transfer-learning properties into a single claim, broadening the scope of the interpretability claim by adding related functionality.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CpG Transformer illustrates rapid transfer learning properties, allowing practitioners to train models on new datasets with a limited computational and time budget.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CpG Transformer demonstrates interpretability and rapid transfer learning properties.
alignments.rationale: The transfer-learning property is preserved in the publication claim, which simply bundles it with interpretability; the content and confidence remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The EntireAxon convolutional neural network sensitively and specifically segmented the features of AxD, and its performance exceeded that of human expert raters.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The trained convolutional neural network (CNN) sensitively and specifically segmented the features of axonal degeneration (AxD), outperforming human evaluators.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates that the trained CNN segmented AxD features with sensitivity/specificity and outperformed humans, matching the preprint claim directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In an in vitro model of AxD induced by hemin, we detected a concentration- and time-dependent degeneration of axons leading to a decrease in axon area.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In an in vitro model of AxD induced by hemorrhagic stroke, a time-dependent degeneration of axons was detected, characterized by a decrease in axon area and an increase in axonal swelling and fragment areas.
alignments.rationale: The core observation (time-dependent degeneration and decreased axon area) is preserved, while the publication adds context (increased swellings/fragments) and slightly modifies wording about the inducing condition.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Axonal swellings preceded axon fragmentation, suggesting that swellings may be reliable predictors of AxD.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Axonal swellings preceded axon fragmentation, suggesting that swellings may be reliable predictors of AxD.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this temporal/predictive observation verbatim, with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified four morphological patterns of AxD (granular, retraction, swelling, and transport degeneration) in cortical axons subjected to hemin.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Four morphological patterns of AxD (granular, retraction, swelling, and transport degeneration) were identified using a recurrent neural network (RNN).
alignments.rationale: The identified four patterns are the same in both versions; the publication mentions an RNN for identification whereas the preprint emphasized the EntireAxon CNN, but the reported finding itself is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings indicate a morphological heterogeneity of AxD under pathophysiologic conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The EntireAxon platform enables systematic analysis of axons and AxD in time-lapse microscopy, revealing a previously unknown intricacy of AxD in a disease context.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the conclusion to emphasize the platform capability and asserts discovery of previously unknown intricacy, which strengthens/expands the interpretive emphasis relative to the preprint's more neutral statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We have developed a new systems-biology-informed deep learning algorithm that incorporates the system of ordinary differential equations into the neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new systems-biology-informed deep learning algorithm has been developed that incorporates ordinary differential equations into neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the development of the ODE-incorporating systems-biology-informed algorithm with the same meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using few scattered and noisy measurements, we are able to infer the dynamics of unobserved species, external forcing, and the unknown model parameters.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using few scattered and noisy measurements, the algorithm can infer the dynamics of unobserved species, external forcing, and unknown model parameters.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this capability claim verbatim and at the same level of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: By incorporating the system of ODEs into the neural networks, we effectively add constraints to the optimization algorithm, which makes the method robust to noisy and sparse measurements.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm effectively adds constraints to the optimization procedure, imposing structure on the observational data.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the constraint/structure effect on the optimization procedure; the robustness interpretation is framed more as imposed structure, clarifying the mechanism without changing the substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We have successfully tested the algorithm for three different benchmark problems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm has been successfully tested for three different benchmark problems.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the benchmarking claim directly and with equivalent confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MetaPathPredict is a software tool that incorporates machine learning models to predict the presence of complete KEGG modules within bacterial genomic datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MetaPathPredict is a software tool that incorporates machine learning models to predict the presence of complete KEGG modules within bacterial genomic datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the software tool claim identically in scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MetaPathPredict employs neural network and XGBoost stacked ensemble models to reconstruct and predict the presence of KEGG modules in a genome.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MetaPathPredict employs deep learning models to predict the presence of KEGG modules in a genome using gene annotation data and information from the KEGG module database.
alignments.rationale: The publication changes the methodological description (from an explicit NN+XGBoost ensemble to a general 'deep learning models') and adds emphasis on input data sources, altering the original methodological specificity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MetaPathPredict makes robust predictions of KEGG module presence within highly incomplete genomes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Benchmarks show that MetaPathPredict makes robust predictions of KEGG module presence within highly incomplete genomes.
alignments.rationale: The publication confirms the benchmarking result directly, preserving the robustness claim and its scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A pipeline for cell segmentation and quantification using machine learning-based pixel classification has been developed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MIRIAM combines a pipeline for cell segmentation and quantification with machine learning-based pixel classification to define cellular compartments.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the pipeline claim and adds that it is used to define cellular compartments, a mild clarification rather than a substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method includes a novel approach for extending incomplete cell membranes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MIRIAM includes a novel method for extending incomplete cell membranes.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim with equivalent wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The pipeline allows for quantification of antibody staining in human colorectal adenomas.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MIRIAM is superior to widely utilized segmentation methods when applied to human colonic adenomas.
alignments.rationale: The preprint reports the pipeline's use on human adenomas; the publication reframes results to claim superiority over other methods, which is a stronger, broader assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning-based cell shape descriptor is part of the proposed segmentation method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MIRIAM utilizes a deep learning-based cell shape descriptor.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same contribution about including a deep learning-based cell shape descriptor.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This method can be broadly applied to different imaging platforms and tissue types.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MIRIAM provides a pipeline that is broadly applicable to different imaging platforms and tissue types.
alignments.rationale: Both texts assert broad applicability with similar certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning models can automatically distinguish between caveolae and scaffolds from single molecule localization microscopy (SMLM) data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning models can automatically distinguish between caveolae and scaffolds from single molecule localization microscopy (SMLM) data.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The random forest classifier and deep learning approach achieve classification accuracy reaching 94% on the test set.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The random forest classifier and deep learning approach achieve classification accuracy of 94% in distinguishing caveolae from non-caveolae structures.
alignments.rationale: The numeric accuracy and methods are reported the same way in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The PointNet model achieved a classification accuracy of only 83% in distinguishing caveolae from non-caveolae structures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The PointNet model achieves a classification accuracy of 83% in identifying caveolae compared to other methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the same 83% result but situates it relative to other methods and lists a lower certainty (medium), indicating a mild clarification and tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work is the first to leverage machine learning approaches to automatically identify biological structures from SMLM data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work is the first to leverage machine learning approaches to automatically identify biological structures from SMLM data.
alignments.rationale: The novelty claim is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MCGLPPI achieves competitive performance compared with existing algorithms at the atom- and residue-scale.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MCGLPPI achieves competitive performance in predicting protein-protein interaction properties at the coarse-grained scale compared to atom- and residue-scale methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the coarse-grained context while preserving the core claim of competitive performance versus atom- and residue-scale methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MCGLPPI requires only a third of the computational resources compared to traditional methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MCGLPPI requires only a third of the computational resources compared to existing atom- and residue-scale algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The resource-efficiency claim is reiterated with equivalent magnitude and comparison set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CG-scale pre-training on protein domain-domain interaction structures enhances the predictive capabilities for PPI tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Coarse-grained pre-training on protein domain-domain interaction structures enhances the predictive capabilities of MCGLPPI for protein-protein interaction tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same mechanism/result about coarse-grained pre-training improving predictive power.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MCGLPPI combines graph neural networks with the MARTINI molecular coarse-grained model to predict overall PPI properties accurately and efficiently.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MCGLPPI serves as a promising tool for the large-scale analysis of biomolecular interactions.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's method-level contribution is reframed in the publication as a broader conclusion about being a promising large-scale tool, expanding the claimed applicability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning can predict critical quality attributes and process parameters for microfluidic-based liposome production.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning can predict critical quality attributes and process parameters for microfluidic-based liposome production.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same predictive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Validated models significantly advance our understanding of lipid behaviour during microfluidic liposomal production.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Validated models significantly advance our understanding of lipid behaviour during microfluidic liposomal production.
alignments.rationale: The claim about validated models and understanding lipid behaviour is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of artificial intelligence in predicting lipid behaviour has the potential to streamline the development of drug delivery systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of artificial intelligence in predicting lipid behaviour has the potential to streamline the development of drug delivery systems.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about AI's potential in drug delivery is restated identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive model analysis enhances interpretability and investigates underlying mechanisms in microfluidic production.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive model analysis enhances interpretability and investigates underlying mechanisms in microfluidic production.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim about model analysis and interpretability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Unlocking the potential of machine learning in drug development can accelerate pharmaceutical innovation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Unlocking the potential of machine learning in drug development can accelerate pharmaceutical innovation.
alignments.rationale: The high-level conclusion about accelerating pharmaceutical innovation is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The EfficientNet neural network can predict the sex of sociable weavers with an accuracy of 76%, which is considerably higher than human performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The EfficientNet neural network can predict the sex of sociable weavers with an accuracy of 76%, which is significantly higher than the 56% accuracy of humans.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the 76% result but quantifies human performance (56%), strengthening and clarifying the comparison.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model was similarly good at predicting females and males.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model demonstrates similar accuracy in predicting both female and male sociable weavers.
alignments.rationale: Both statements convey the same balanced accuracy across sexes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Younger males are less masculine than older ones, and older females are more masculine than younger ones, indicating an interaction of sex with age on the confidence score of the models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Younger males are less masculine than older males, while older females are more masculine than younger females, indicating an interaction between sex and age affecting confidence scores.
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the phrasing to explicitly compare within sexes, clarifying the interaction without changing the substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model mostly uses the beak region to predict the sex of individuals.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Grad-CAM visualization technique revealed that the model primarily used the bill region to differentiate the sexes of sociable weavers.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the method (Grad-CAM) and uses 'bill' vs 'beak' terminology, a clarification of how the conclusion was reached.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Artificial intelligence has the potential to be a non-invasive sexing tool, surpassing human capabilities and aiding in identifying potential cryptic dimorphic body parts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study highlights the potential of artificial intelligence as a noninvasive tool for sexing species that appear monomorphic to humans.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the noninvasive potential claim but removes explicit language about surpassing humans and narrows the emphasis to species appearing monomorphic, reducing the strength of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: partialS/HIC demonstrates excellent resolution for detecting partial sweeps in population genomic data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: partialS/HIC demonstrates excellent resolution for detecting partial sweeps in population genomic data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result claim verbatim with identical certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of partialS/HIC to whole genomes from eight mosquito populations reveals a strong overrepresentation of sweeps at insecticide resistance loci. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of partialS/HIC to whole genomes from eight mosquito populations reveals a strong overrepresentation of sweeps at insecticide resistance loci. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this population-genomic result exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: partialS/HIC introduces a method to distinguish between completed and partial sweeps, as well as between hard and soft sweeps. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: partialS/HIC introduces a method to distinguish between completed and partial sweeps, as well as between hard and soft sweeps. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The methodological/contribution claim is identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis provides a list of candidate adaptive loci that may be relevant to mosquito control efforts. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis provides a list of candidate adaptive loci that may be relevant to mosquito control efforts. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes the same conclusion and candidate locus list with the same emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A deep learning approach with minimal well-annotated training samples can identify multicellular rosettes from 3D live images of the Caenorhabditis elegans embryo with over 80% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method can identify rosettes with more than 80% accuracy using only 10-15 well-labeled rosette images and 30-40 randomly selected nonrosette images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the >80% accuracy claim but adds concrete sample-size details, clarifying the original preprint result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed strategy combines feature transfer and generative adversarial networks (GANs) to enhance image classification with small training samples. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GAN-based transfer approach captures more than 90% of the model accuracy achieved with a training dataset that is five times larger. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses on the GAN-based transfer implementation and reports its performance; this clarifies and provides empirical support for the proposed strategy in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The GAN-based transfer approach can capture over 90% of the model accuracy achieved with a training dataset that is five times larger. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GAN-based transfer approach captures more than 90% of the model accuracy achieved with a training dataset that is five times larger. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This performance claim is restated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A public benchmark dataset for rosette detection has been established. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A public benchmark dataset for rosette detection has been established. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes the same dataset contribution; certainty label is similar (medium/high) and the claim content is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Aging acceleration is elevated in patients with major diseases and is predictive of mortality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific claim about elevated aging acceleration predicting mortality is not present in the publication and appears to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Application of supervised biological age models improves the accuracy of chronological age estimation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Supervised approaches produce better chronological age estimations but lose the association between aging acceleration and all-cause mortality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes the same point about improved chronological age estimation but pairs it with an additional observation about loss of association, thus clarifying the original preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The association between aging acceleration predicted by the model and all-cause mortality is lost when using supervised approaches. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Supervised approaches produce better chronological age estimations but lose the association between aging acceleration and all-cause mortality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the loss-of-association result; the publication presents it with higher certainty, but the substantive claim is the same (I mark confidence as Tempered because the framing emphasizes the outcome alongside improved estimation).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A deep convolution neural network trained to predict mortality risks outperformed biological age and simple linear proportional hazards models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A separate deep CNN for mortality risks prediction outperformed biological age models and a simple linear proportional hazards model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes the same comparative performance result for the deep CNN.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study introduces a novel way to train parametric proportional hazards models suitable for modern machine learning software. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel way to train parametric proportional hazards models was introduced for out-of-the-box implementation with modern machine learning software. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same methodological contribution and frames it with higher certainty and implementation emphasis, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning methods, particularly Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), can be effectively applied to population genetic tasks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep-learning methods, particularly Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), can be effectively applied to population genetic tasks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this high-level conclusion without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The designed GAN is capable of generating artificial examples of population genetic alignments that mimic key aspects of the training data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The designed GAN is capable of generating artificial examples of population genetic alignments that successfully mimic key aspects of the training data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The generation capability claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The GAN demonstrates consistent training success across various evolutionary models without the need for extensive hyperparameter tuning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GAN demonstrates consistent training success across various evolutionary models without the need for extensive hyperparameter tuning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same training-stability/result claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings highlight the ability of GANs to learn and mimic population genetic data, suggesting future applications in population genetics research. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings highlight the ability of GANs to learn and mimic population genetic data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same concluding observation about GANs' capabilities and potential applications.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepSSV, a deep learning-based tool, employs a convolutional neural network (CNN) model to learn increasingly abstract feature representations from raw sequencing data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepSSV, a deep learning-based tool, employs a convolutional neural network model to learn increasingly abstract feature representations from raw sequencing data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The methodological description is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepSSV creates a spatially-oriented representation of read alignments around candidate somatic sites, enabling it to effectively gather scattered evidences. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepSSV creates a spatially oriented representation of read alignments around candidate somatic sites, enabling it to effectively gather scattered evidence. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes the same contribution claim about spatial representation of reads.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The benchmarking results demonstrate that DeepSSV outperforms its state-of-the-art competitors in overall F1 score. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The benchmarking results demonstrate that DeepSSV outperforms its state-of-the-art competitors in overall F1 score. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance benchmarking claim is preserved without change in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An LSTM model named LSTM_Pep was created to generate de novo peptides with potential therapeutic effects. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The LSTM model (LSTM_Pep) has been built and fine-tuned to generate de novo peptides with specific prospective therapeutic benefits. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution but adds that the model was fine-tuned; this refines the original claim without changing its scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A deep learning-based protein-peptide prediction model named DeepPep was developed for fast screening of generated peptides. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A deep learning-based protein-peptide prediction model (DeepPep) has been developed for rapid screening of generated peptides for given targets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same development and specifies target-oriented rapid screening, clarifying usage without changing scope or reported confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The iterative finetuning of training, generating, and screening peptides can achieve higher predicted binding affinity peptides. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The work demonstrates that iteratively fine-tuning training, generating, and screening peptides can achieve higher-predicted binding affinity peptides. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this iterative-finetuning result almost verbatim, keeping scope and confidence the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The work demonstrates the potential of deep learning-based methods to effectively generate bioactive peptides with specific therapeutic effects. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning can be used to generate a large number of potentially bioactive peptides, similar to the generation of de novo chemical compounds. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the same overall conclusion but emphasizes large-scale generation and draws an analogy to chemical compound generation, slightly broadening the claim's emphasis while retaining confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study presents a large collection of annotated cochlear hair-cell datasets, labeled with commonly used hair-cell markers. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study presents a large collection of annotated cochlear hair-cell datasets, labeled with commonly used hair-cell markers. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution verbatim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset includes over 90,000 hair cells, all of which have been manually identified and annotated as one of two cell types: inner hair cells and outer hair cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset includes over 107,000 hair cells identified and annotated as either inner or outer hair cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a substantially larger cell count (90k -> 107k), revising the numeric result and broadening the dataset size while keeping the claim type and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This dataset can facilitate the development of generalizable cochlear hair cell detection models or serve as a starting point for fine-tuning models for other analysis tasks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This dataset can facilitate the development of generalizable cochlear hair-cell detection models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same conclusion about facilitating model development but separates out the "starting point for fine-tuning" into an additional claim; here the core facilitation claim is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The collection represents a variety of imaging techniques and includes samples from multiple species and conditions. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not mention the diversity of imaging techniques, species, or conditions, so this preprint claim appears omitted in the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The BTFBS model predicts whether bacterial transcription factors and binding sites combine with high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The BTFBS model predicts whether bacterial transcription factors and binding sites combine with high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result directly with no substantive changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: On the test dataset of RS, the BTFBS model achieved an accuracy of 0.91446. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: On the test dataset of RS, the BTFBS model achieved an accuracy of 0.91446. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric performance result is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The optimal approach for obtaining negative samples in bacterial research is to utilize the whole genome sequences of the corresponding bacteria. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The optimal approach for obtaining negative samples in bacterial research is to utilize the whole genome sequences of the corresponding bacteria. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The methodological recommendation is carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The BTFBS model provides an experimental guide for predicting the combination of bacterial transcription factors and their binding sites. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The BTFBS model provides a good performance and can serve as an experimental guide. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit performance judgment ('good performance') while preserving the claim that the model can serve as an experimental guide, clarifying but not changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DARDN is a computational method that utilizes convolutional neural networks for predicting cancer-specific CTCF binding sites from long DNA sequences. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DARDN demonstrates the ability to classify DNA sequences surrounding cancer-specific CTCF binding from control constitutive CTCF binding. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the demonstrated classification ability rather than describing the method design; this reframes the contribution as an demonstrated result, clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Evaluation on DNA sequences associated with CTCF binding sites in T-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia and other cancer types demonstrates DARDN’s ability in classifying DNA sequences surrounding cancer-specific CTCF binding from control constitutive CTCF binding. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DARDN demonstrates the ability to classify DNA sequences surrounding cancer-specific CTCF binding from control constitutive CTCF binding. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This result is restated directly in the publication and remains substantively the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DARDN identifies sequence motifs for transcription factors potentially active in each specific cancer type. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method identifies sequence motifs for transcription factors potentially active in each specific cancer type. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the motif-identification result but states it with higher certainty; the core claim is clarified and the reported confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identified potential oncogenic transcription factors in T-ALL, AML, BRCA, CRC, LUAD, and PRAD. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identify potential oncogenic transcription factors in T-ALL, AML, BRCA, CRC, LUAD, and PRAD. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this list of identified potential oncogenic transcription factors verbatim, with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The work demonstrates the power of advanced machine learning and feature discovery approaches in finding biologically meaningful information from complex high-throughput sequencing data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate this broad methodological claim and instead adds a limitation about long sequences; the original high-level conclusion is not present in the listed publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed brain MRI-based AD diagnostic classifier achieved 90.9% accuracy in leave-sites-out cross-validation on the Alzheimer’s Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (ADNI) dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed brain MRI-based AD diagnostic classifier achieved 90.9% accuracy in leave-sites-out cross-validation on the Alzheimer’s Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (ADNI) dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The cross-validation accuracy result is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classifier demonstrated 94.5% accuracy on the AIBL dataset, 93.6% on the MIRIAD dataset, and 91.1% on the OASIS dataset for direct tests on three unseen independent datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AD classifier demonstrated 94.5%, 93.6%, and 91.1% accuracy for direct tests on three unseen independent datasets (AIBL, MIRIAD, OASIS). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats these independent-test accuracies verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mild cognitive impairment (MCI) patients who converted to Alzheimer’s disease (AD) were 3 times more likely to be predicted as AD than those who did not convert. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mild cognitive impairment (MCI) patients who converted to Alzheimer’s disease (AD) were 3 times more likely to be predicted as AD than those who did not convert. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is the same but the publication reports higher certainty, indicating amplification of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predicted scores from the AD classifier showed significant correlations with illness severity. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predicted scores from the AD classifier showed significant correlations with illness severity. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the correlation result with the same framing and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed AD classifier has the potential to be integrated into AD diagnostic practice as a medical-grade marker. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed AD classifier has potential to be integrated into AD diagnostic practice. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the integration potential but softens the language (drops 'medical-grade' and reduces certainty), so the claim is clarified and its confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Grammaticality can be a measure of protein viability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Grammaticality can be a measure of protein viability.
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is verbatim the same assertion about grammaticality as a measure of protein viability, so no substantive change occurred.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The traditional metric ΔΔ G appears to be more effective than grammaticality for measuring protein viability.
