alignments.pub_claim_text: Interpretation of neural net responses in the trained CNN model highlights Raman spectral ranges that are most important to virus identification. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Prompt enhancement methods that utilize explicit signals in user queries improve LLM-generated responses in specialized domains such as biology and biomedicine. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method achieved a median answer quality score of 2.5, surpassing both the baseline model and the text embedding-based approach. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach addresses the shortcomings of traditional text embedding-based prompt enhancement methods. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: This study highlights the potential of deep learning models for deciphering the complex interplay between epigenomic features and 3D genome organization. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Glial cells play a crucial role in modulating synaptic activity, especially in synchronous networks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TurNuP is a general and organism-independent model that successfully predicts turnover numbers for natural reactions of wild-type enzymes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TurNuP is a general and organism-independent model that successfully predicts turnover numbers for natural reactions of wild-type enzymes.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Parameterizing metabolic models with TurNuP-predicted kcat values leads to improved proteome allocation predictions.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same result about improved proteome allocation when using TurNuP predictions.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The new perspective on reward-based learning accommodates the findings that motivated the standard model while addressing a wider range of observations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This finding demonstrates a novel application of AI to medical imaging. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This finding demonstrates a novel application of AI to medical imaging. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Seqgra is a deep learning pipeline that incorporates rule-based simulation of biological sequence data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our model, Jenever, produces accurate haplotypes with correct phase and genotype for all classes of small variants. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Jenever demonstrates superior overall accuracy compared to FreeBayes, GATK HaplotypeCaller, Clair3, and DeepVariant. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Jenever demonstrates superior overall accuracy compared to FreeBayes, GATK HaplotypeCaller, Clair3, and DeepVariant. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Directly matching statement; no substantive change in claim or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: At F 1-maximizing quality thresholds, Jenever delivers the highest sensitivity, precision, and the fewest genotyping errors for insertion and deletion variants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: At F1-maximizing quality thresholds, Jenever delivers the highest sensitivity, precision, and the fewest genotyping errors for insertion and deletion variants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claim for indels with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For single nucleotide variants, Jenever demonstrates the highest sensitivity but at somewhat lower precision, achieving the highest overall F 1 score among all callers tested. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: For single nucleotide variants, Jenever achieves the highest overall F1 score among all callers tested. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication removes the caveat about lower precision and increases stated certainty, changing the nuance of the SNV performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learning process of thermal soaring has learning bottlenecks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learning process of thermal soaring has learning bottlenecks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new efficiency metric was defined to characterize learning robustness in thermal soaring. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new efficiency metric was defined to characterize learning robustness in thermal soaring. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Same contribution claim appears unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learned policy from the deep-RL system was compared to data from soaring vultures. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learned policy from the deep-RL system was compared to data from soaring vultures. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Comparison to vulture data is reported identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neurons of the trained network divide into function clusters that evolve during learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neurons of the trained network divide into function clusters that evolve during learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Neural clustering result unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Thermal soaring is posed as a rich yet tractable model-problem for the learning of motion control. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Thermal soaring is posed as a rich yet tractable model-problem for the learning of motion control. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion retained verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The K-nearest neighbour algorithm was able to predict 78% of the upcoming tail biting events with an accuracy of 96% when using data from known pens. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The K-nearest neighbour algorithm was able to predict 78% of the upcoming tail biting events with an accuracy of 96%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication omits the qualifier 'when using data from known pens', effectively broadening the claim to a more general statement of performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural network model detected 14% of the upcoming tail biting events with an accuracy of 63% when predicting for unknown pens. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific result for unknown pens is not present in the publication and thus appears to have been removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning models can be considered for implementation into automatic feeder systems for real-time prediction of tail biting events. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning models can be considered for implementation into automatic feeder systems for real-time prediction of tail biting events. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion appears verbatim but the stated certainty is reduced from high to medium in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study evaluated the prediction capacities of seven machine learning algorithms using feeding behaviour data from 65 pens of grower-finisher pigs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This methodological detail about the study design and scale does not appear in the provided preprint claim list and is thus an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We present a fast and accurate deep learning approach for viral host prediction based solely on the viral genome sequence. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We present a fast and accurate deep learning approach for viral host prediction based solely on the viral genome sequence. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our deep neural network achieved an AUG between 0.93 and 0.98 for each virus species tested, outperforming previous approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our deep neural network achieved an AUC between 0.93 and 0.98 for three different virus species, allowing highly accurate predictions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the AUC range and species tested but drops the explicit statement about outperforming previous approaches, changing the asserted comparative claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep neural networks are suitable for predicting the host of a virus, even with a limited amount of sequences and highly unbalanced available data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep neural networks are suitable to predict the host of a virus, even with a limited amount of sequences and highly unbalanced available data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion preserved with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The trained models for the prediction of the host for influenza A, rabies lyssavirus, and rotavirus A are implemented in the tool VIDHOP. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The trained DNNs are the core of our virus-host prediction tool VIrus Deep learning HOst Prediction (VIDHOP). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both statements indicate the trained models are part of the VIDHOP tool; wording differs but meaning is the same.

alignments.pub_claim_text: VIDHOP allows the user to train and use models for other viruses. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This feature/claim about VIDHOP's extensibility is not present in the preprint claim list and appears as an additional publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method for generating protein domain sequences intended to interact with another protein domain outperforms state-of-the-art shallow auto-regressive strategies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method for generating protein domain sequences intended to interact with another protein domain outperforms state-of-the-art shallow autoregressive strategies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Result claim is the same; only minor orthographic difference (autoregressive/autoregressive).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We develop a method that casts the problem of generating artificial partner sequences as a translation problem from a given interactor domain to the new domain to be generated. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method can generate artificial partner sequences conditional on an input sequence. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication restates the contribution in more general/concise terms (conditional generation) rather than the explicit 'translation' framing in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We explore the possibility of fine-tuning pre-trained large language models for the task of generating protein domain sequences. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fine-tuning pretrained large language models can be explored for the task of generating protein domain sequences. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The exploratory conclusion about fine-tuning LLMs is preserved with essentially identical meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We assess the quality of sampled sequences using Alphafold 2. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The quality of sampled sequences can be assessed using Alphafold 2. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Assessment using AlphaFold2 is maintained; phrasing slightly different but same conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deeper and narrower autoencoders generally lead to better imputation performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deeper and narrower autoencoders generally lead to better imputation performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is word-for-word the same as the preprint claim with equal certainty and result framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sigmoid and tanh activation functions consistently outperform other commonly used functions including ReLU. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The sigmoid and tanh activation functions consistently outperform other commonly used functions including ReLU. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about activation functions is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Regularization improves the accuracy of imputation and downstream cell clustering and DE gene analyses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Regularization improves the accuracy of imputation and downstream cell clustering and DE gene analyses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result about regularization with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results differ from common practices in the computer vision field regarding the activation function and the regularization strategy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our results differ from common practices in the computer vision field regarding the activation function and the regularization strategy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim about divergence from computer vision practices is unchanged in text and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Many well-known perceptual and neural phenomena are present in a deep neural network optimized for object recognition. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Many perceptual phenomena, including the Thatcher effect, mirror confusion, Weber’s law, relative size, multiple object normalization, and correlated sparseness, emerged after object recognition training in deep neural networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies which phenomena and that they emerged after object-recognition training, clarifying the original general statement without changing its thrust.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Some perceptual phenomena, such as processing of 3D shape and occlusion, are notably absent in the deep neural network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Certain perceptual phenomena, such as 3D shape processing, surface invariance, occlusion, natural parts, and the global advantage, were absent in trained deep neural networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication lists the same absent phenomena (including the examples named in the preprint), preserving the original claim's meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Learning to recognize objects suffices to produce some perceptual phenomena but not others. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings indicate sufficient conditions for the emergence of perceptual phenomena in both brains and deep networks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication couches the idea more cautiously as indicating sufficient conditions and extends to both brains and networks, reducing certainty relative to the preprint's stronger claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results reveal perceptual properties that could be incorporated into deep networks to improve their performance. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study offers clues to the properties that could be incorporated to improve deep networks. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the contribution in very similar terms about properties that could improve networks.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Some perceptual phenomena, such as the global advantage effect, sparseness, and relative size, were present in randomly initialized deep neural networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This result about phenomena present in randomly initialized networks was not in the preprint and is introduced newly in the publication, so it is marked as an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed adaptive closed-loop control framework successfully accelerates the wound healing process by 17.71%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach successfully accelerated the wound-healing process by 17.71%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric acceleration and result wording are preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The data-driven model estimates a 40% acceleration in wound closure. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the 40% estimate; instead it omits this quantitative claim, so the preprint claim appears removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach eliminates the need for mathematical modeling of complex nonlinear wound healing dynamics. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not make the strong claim of eliminating mathematical modeling, so this contribution from the preprint was not carried forward.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Real-time monitoring allows for precise adjustment in treatment strategies that align with an individual’s unique biological response. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed adaptive closed-loop control framework integrates deep learning, optimal control, and reinforcement learning to update treatment strategies in real time. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains real-time updating but frames it as an integrated framework (less explicitly claiming precise, individualized alignment), so the claim is clarified and somewhat tempered.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The data-driven model suggests that the treatment strategy results in an accelerated onset of inflammation and subsequent transition to proliferation in a porcine wound model. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The mechanistic observation about inflammation and proliferation in a porcine model is new in the publication and was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The µ Protein framework accelerates protein engineering by combining a deep learning model with a reinforcement learning algorithm. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The μProtein framework accelerates protein engineering by combining a deep learning model with a reinforcement learning algorithm. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution description (orthographic μ/µ difference aside) with the same emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: µ Protein identified high-gain-of-function multi-point mutants for the enzyme β-lactamase, surpassing the highest known activity level. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: μProtein identified high-gain-of-function multi-point mutants for the enzyme β-lactamase, surpassing one of the highest-known activity levels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication weakens the superlative by changing 'the highest known' to 'one of the highest-known' activity levels, narrowing and tempering the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: µ Protein demonstrates the capability to discover impactful mutations across vast protein sequence space. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: μProtein demonstrates the capability to discover impactful mutations across the vast protein sequence space. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the general concluding claim about discovering impactful mutations across sequence space.

alignments.pub_claim_text: μProtein leverages single-mutation data to predict optimal sequences with complex, multi-amino-acid mutations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit methodological detail about leveraging single-mutation data that was not stated in the preprint, so this is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach identifies potential ALS-associated genetic variants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach identifies potentially ALS-associated promoter regions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the locus of interest from general genetic variants to promoter regions specifically, clarifying and narrowing the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method generally outperforms other classification methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method generally outperforms other classification methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Test results support the hypothesis that ALS is caused by non-additive combinations of variants. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Test results support the hypothesis that non-additive combinations of variants contribute to ALS. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication softens causal language ('caused by' -> 'contribute to'), revising the strength of the causal claim while keeping the same general finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We consider this a first step towards genotype-phenotype association with deep learning that is tailored to genomics. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The architectures and protocols developed are tailored toward processing population-scale, whole-genome data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution more concretely as architectures/protocols for population-scale whole-genome data (broader and more concrete) and presents it with higher certainty than the preprint's 'first step' wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sherlock, a novel algorithm, can reduce the time required to process camera trap data by removing a large number of unwanted images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sherlock, a novel algorithm, can reduce the time required to process camera trap data by removing a large number of unwanted images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim with identical certainty and scope, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm correctly classified 91.9% of badger images and removed 49.3% of the unwanted ‘empty’ images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm correctly classified 91.9% of badger images and removed 49.3% of the unwanted ‘empty’ images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical results and phrasing are identical between preprint and publication, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Faster processing times were achieved by reducing both the number of sampled pixels and 'bouncing' attempts, with minimal implications for model sensitivity and specificity. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Faster processing times were achieved by reducing both the number of sampled pixels and ‘bouncing’ attempts, with minimal implications for model sensitivity and specificity. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same mechanism and effect with equivalent certainty, indicating no semantic change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When tested on two sites with no livestock images, Sherlock's performance improved, removing 92.3% of the empty images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: When tested on two sites which contained no livestock in their images, Sherlock's performance greatly improved and it removed 92.3% of the empty images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same site-specific result and numeric outcome, with only minor wording differences (e.g., 'greatly improved').

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sherlock is currently an accessible, simple, and useful tool for processing camera trap data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sherlock is currently an accessible, simple and useful tool for processing camera trap data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about accessibility and usefulness is repeated without substantive change in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep hybrid models demonstrate a systematic generalization improvement over shallow hybrid models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep hybrid models demonstrate a systematic generalization improvement over shallow counterparts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same high-level result (generalization improvement) using nearly identical wording ('shallow counterparts'), so meaning and confidence are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CPU cost to train deep hybrid models is lower than that for shallow hybrid models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CPU cost to train deep hybrid models is lower than that for shallow counterparts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about lower CPU training cost is carried into the publication with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In the pilot 50L MUT+ Pichia pastoris dataset, the prediction accuracy increased by 18.4% and the CPU time decreased by 43.4%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In the pilot 50L MUT+ Pichia pastoris data set, prediction accuracy increased by 18.4% and CPU time decreased by 43.4%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The dataset-specific numerical improvements are reproduced verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning techniques such as ADAM and stochastic regularization significantly reduce the training CPU time. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The general methodological claim about ADAM and stochastic regularization reducing CPU time does not appear among the publication claims; it seems omitted in the published summary.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TickIDNet scores an 87.8% identification accuracy across all three species of ticks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The convolutional neural network 'TickIDNet' achieves an identification accuracy of 87.8% across three common tick species. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TickIDNet outperforms the accuracy of identifications done by a member of the general public or healthcare professionals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TickIDNet outperforms the accuracy of identifications done by the general public or healthcare professionals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The comparative performance claim is preserved with equivalent targets and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Image quality, particularly the size of the tick in the image, significantly affects the network’s ability to correctly identify an image. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Image quality, particularly the size of the tick in the image, significantly affects the network's ability to correctly identify ticks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same limitation about image quality and tick size affecting performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TickIDNet's performance can be improved by using confidence thresholds and building image submission pipelines for better quality photos. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of TickIDNet can be improved by implementing confidence thresholds and encouraging better quality photo submissions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning represents a promising frontier for tick identification that should be further explored and deployed. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning represents a promising frontier for tick identification that should be further explored to address public health issues related to tick-borne diseases. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A federated trained model to segment interstitial fibrosis and tubular atrophy (IFTA) using datasets from three institutions is comparable to a model trained by pooling the data on one server. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Federated training to segment interstitial fibrosis and tubular atrophy (IFTA) using datasets from three institutions is not found to be different from training by pooling the data on one server. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same result (no difference between federated and pooled training) using different wording but equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Training a model to segment glomeruli for a federated dataset (split by staining) demonstrates similar performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Training a model to segment glomeruli for a federated dataset (split by staining) demonstrates similar performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The glomeruli segmentation result is reproduced identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The collection and curation of diverse training datasets is the largest bottleneck to the development of convolutional neural network (CNN) models in the computational pathology domain. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The collection and curation of diverse training datasets is the largest bottleneck to the development of convolutional neural network (CNN) models in the computational pathology domain. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion about dataset collection and curation being the main bottleneck with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AB-Gen method can learn the antibody space of heavy chain complementarity determining region 3 (CDRH3) and generate sequences with similar property distributions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AB-Gen method can learn the antibody space of heavy chain complementarity determining region 3 (CDRH3) and generate sequences with similar property distributions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is word-for-word the same as the preprint claim, so there is no semantic, scope, or confidence change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The agent model of AB-Gen was able to generate novel CDRH3 sequences that fulfill multi-property constraints when using HER2 as the target. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The agent model of AB-Gen was able to generate novel CDRH3 sequences that fulfill multi-property constraints when using human epidermal growth factor receptor-2 (HER2) as the target. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with expanded receptor naming (HER2); the core claim and confidence remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 509 generated sequences were able to pass all property filters, and three highly conserved residues were identified. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A total of 509 generated sequences were able to pass all property filters, and three highly conserved residues were identified. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same numeric result and identification of residues with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The importance of the three highly conserved residues was further demonstrated by molecular dynamics simulations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The importance of the three highly conserved residues was further demonstrated by molecular dynamics simulations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this result verbatim, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AB-Gen method is able to design novel antibody sequences with an improved success rate than the traditional propose-then-filter approach. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AB-Gen method is able to design novel antibody sequences with an improved success rate than the traditional propose-then-filter approach. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about improved success rate is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SlumberNet achieved high levels of accuracy (∼98%) in predicting sleep stages. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SlumberNet achieved high levels of overall performance with an accuracy of 97% and an F1 score of 96% in predicting sleep stages. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides more precise performance metrics (97% accuracy, F1=96%) versus the preprint's ~98%; this refines the claim and slightly lowers the stated accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SlumberNet showed robust performance even with a small and diverse training dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SlumberNet showed robust performance even with a small and diverse training dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This claim is reproduced exactly in the publication with the same emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SlumberNet resulted in a significant reduction in analysis time (∼50x faster) compared to manual sleep stage classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SlumberNet provided a significant reduction in analysis time, being approximately 50 times faster than manual sleep stage classification without sacrificing accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same speed improvement but clarifies that this speedup did not come at the cost of accuracy, adding supporting detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SlumberNet has the potential to be adapted to human EEG analysis and sleep stage classification. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study showcases the potential of deep learning to facilitate sleep research by providing a more efficient, accurate, and scalable method for sleep stage classification. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication broadens the preprint's specific adaptation-to-human-EEG claim into a more general statement about facilitating sleep research and scalable methods, shifting emphasis from a concrete adaptation to broader applicability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SlumberNet could be a valuable tool in understanding both sleep physiology and disorders in mammals. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study showcases the potential of deep learning to facilitate sleep research by providing a more efficient, accurate, and scalable method for sleep stage classification. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the broader utility for sleep research (including physiology/disorders) in terms of methodological facilitation, clarifying how SlumberNet could be valuable without explicitly repeating the preprint's mammal-specific phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning can be effectively adapted for diversification inference from phylogenies associated with trait data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We extend the applicability of deep learning methods to the inference of state-dependent diversification models from phylogenies associated with trait data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same adaptation claim but more specifically as an extension to state-dependent diversification models, clarifying the contribution.
