alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same frequency error (0.058 ± 0.050 Hz at low SNR) that the preprint attributes to the refined model on Off spectra, indicating the numeric result is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CNN-SR+ achieved the best performance when processing the published Big GABA in vivo dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using in vivo datasets, the CNN-SR achieved the best performance with mean errors of 0.000055 ± 0.000054 without offsets and 0.000062 ± 0.000068 at small offsets.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication report that CNN-SR achieves the best performance on in vivo datasets, with publication giving the supporting error metrics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results indicate the utility of using deep learning for spectral registration and demonstrate the application of unsupervised learning in improving model performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed CNN-SR method is an efficient and accurate approach for simultaneous frequency-and-phase correction of single-voxel MEGA-PRESS MRS data.
alignments.rationale: The publication asserts the method's efficiency and accuracy and thus supports the preprint's broader conclusion about the utility of deep learning, though it omits detailed emphasis on unsupervised learning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A large benchmark dataset comprising 126,779 experimentally-supported, linear epitope-containing regions was generated from over 1.3 million B cell assays.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A large benchmark dataset comprising 124,879 experimentally supported linear epitope-containing regions was generated from 3567 protein clusters.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports slightly different dataset counts and a more specific provenance (protein clusters), indicating a revision and narrowing of the dataset description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy in linear BCE prediction varies significantly with different features, with sequence features outperforming structural features.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy in linear BCE prediction varies significantly with different features, and all sequence-derived and structural features were found to be informative.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains that accuracy varies by feature but removes the preprint's strong assertion that sequence features outperform structural ones, instead reporting both feature types as informative.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed deep learning framework, NetBCE, achieved an average area under the curve (AUC) value of 0.846 in five-fold cross validation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: NetBCE achieved high accuracy with an average area under the curve (AUC) value of 0.8455 in five-fold cross-validation.
alignments.rationale: The performance metric is preserved (minor numeric rounding difference) and the claim is maintained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NetBCE outperformed conventional machine learning algorithms and other tools, showing an over 22.06% improvement in AUC value compared to other tools using an independent dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: NetBCE substantially outperformed conventional machine learning algorithms with more than 22.06% improvement of AUC value compared to other tools.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the substantial AUC improvement on the independent dataset with equivalent magnitude and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The NetBCE tool will be useful for linear B-cell epitopes identification and for immunological and computational biology research.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly include this forward-looking utility statement; it focuses on dataset and performance metrics rather than the stated prospective utility.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results from our experiments demonstrate significant performance improvement compared to the current state-of-the-art in Off-Target prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results from our experiments demonstrate significant performance improvement compared with the current state-of-the-art in Off-Target prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is effectively the same statement with only minor wording changes; the meaning, scope, and confidence are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model can be considered as the first model combining high efficacy, interpretability, and a desirable balance between precision and recall. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our model can be considered as the first model combining high efficacy, interpretability and a desirable balance between precision and recall. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim verbatim (minor punctuation change) without altering scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We provide a detailed analysis and understanding of the underlying factors that contribute to Off-Target predictions, particularly the presence of two sub-regions in the seed region of sgRNA. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We provide a detailed analysis and understanding of the underlying factors that contribute to Off-Target predictions, particularly the presence of two sub-regions in the seed region of single guide RNA. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same analysis but expands the abbreviation 'sgRNA' to 'single guide RNA', a minor clarification that does not change scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Previous studies have demonstrated improvement in Off-Target prediction capability with the application of deep learning, but they often struggle with the precision-recall trade-off. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: This contextual limitation about prior work does not appear in the publication's listed claims and thus seems to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The implementation of the attention mechanism in the DeepGRN model improves the performance of deep learning models for binding site prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepGRN can automatically and effectively predict transcription factor binding sites from DNA sequences and DNase-Seq coverage. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint credits performance improvement specifically to the attention mechanism; the publication emphasizes overall model effectiveness without explicitly attributing gains to attention, reducing specificity and apparent strength of the causal claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepGRN achieves better performance in at least 9 of 13 targets compared to any of the methods that participated in the DREAM challenge. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepGRN achieves higher unified scores in 6 of 13 targets than any of the top four methods in the DREAM challenge. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric claim and comparator set are weakened in the publication (9->6 targets and compared to top four methods rather than any participating method), so the claim is substantively revised and narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Visualization of the attention weights reveals how those weights shift when binding signal peaks move along the genomic sequence. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The visualization techniques developed for the attention modules help to interpret how critical patterns from different types of input features are recognized by the model. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the visualization result more generally as enabling interpretation of critical patterns across input features, broadening the specific observation about attention weight shifts.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The attention mechanism helps to extract useful features by focusing on critical regions for successful prediction while ignoring irrelevant signals. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The attention weights learned by the model are correlated with potential informative inputs, such as DNase-Seq coverage and motifs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint asserts a functional mechanism for attention (focusing/ignoring), whereas the publication reports correlations with informative inputs, a weaker, more narrowly stated finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GNN models perform significantly better than the other baseline models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GNN models perform significantly better than the other baseline models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using FC measures to estimate brain graphs improves the performance of GNN compared to models trained using a fixed graph based on the spatial distance between the EEG sensors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using FC measures to estimate brain graphs improves the performance of GNN compared to models trained using a fixed graph based on the spatial distance between the EEG sensors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Statement is reproduced exactly in the publication, preserving meaning and strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: No FC measure performs consistently better than the other measures. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: No FC measure performs consistently better than the other measures. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion that no FC measure is consistently superior is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best GNN reaches 0.984 area under sensitivity-specificity curve (AUC) and 92% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best GNN reaches 0.984 area under sensitivity-specificity curve (AUC) and 92% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numeric performance figures are identical in the publication, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best baseline model, a convolutional neural network, has 0.924 AUC and 84.7% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best baseline model, a convolutional neural network, has 0.924 AUC and 84.7% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The baseline performance numbers and claim are unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepAge, a novel deep learning framework, enhances the prediction of biological age from DNA methylation profiles using selected CpGs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepAge, a novel deep learning framework, enhances the prediction of biological age from DNA methylation profiles using selected CpGs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same core claim; only the label (result vs contribution) differs, not the substantive content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepAge outperforms existing epigenetic clocks across multiple datasets, offering significant improvements in accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepAge outperforms existing epigenetic clocks across multiple datasets, offering significant improvements in accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance superiority claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dual correlation feature selection enhances the model’s predictive capabilities by identifying the most age-relevant CpG sites. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dual correlation feature selection in DeepAge enhances the model's predictive capabilities by identifying the most age-relevant CpG sites. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same claim about the feature selection method improving prediction; only the claim type label changed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method sets a new standard in age estimation and highlights the potential of deep learning in biologically relevant feature extraction. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method sets a new standard in age estimation and highlights the potential of deep learning in biologically relevant feature extraction. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The high-level contribution/impact statement is carried over with no substantive change in meaning or strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recent deep learning models can predict the retention time, ion mobility, and fragment intensities of a peptide from the amino acid sequence with good accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent deep learning models can predict the retention time, ion mobility, and fragment intensities of a peptide from the amino acid sequence with good accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this general statement about DL model capabilities without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AlphaPeptDeep is a modular Python framework that allows non-specialists to create models for predicting peptide properties with minimal coding. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AlphaPeptDeep is a modular Python framework that allows non-specialists to create models for predicting peptide properties with minimal coding. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The description of the framework and its accessibility is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AlphaPeptDeep models for predicting retention time, collisional cross sections, and fragment intensities are at least on par with existing tools. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AlphaPeptDeep models for predicting retention time, collisional cross sections, and fragment intensities are at least on par with existing tools. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the parity-with-existing-tools claim intact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive use of transfer learning in AlphaPeptDeep reduces the need for large data sets to refine models for specific experimental conditions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive use of transfer learning in AlphaPeptDeep reduces the need for large data sets to refine models for specific experimental conditions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this conclusion about transfer learning's benefit without altering its meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AlphaPeptDeep can predict additional sequence-based properties, as demonstrated with a novel HLA peptide prediction model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not include the specific example of the HLA peptide prediction model, so this specific demonstration appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed CNN-based model achieved a median overall F1-score of 0.84 for the 9-type classification on the hidden test set.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed convolution neural network model achieved a median overall F1-score of 0.84 for the nine-type cardiac arrhythmia classification.
alignments.rationale: The numeric performance and target task are the same in preprint and publication with only minor wording differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model ranked first in the AI competition for ECG-based cardiac arrhythmia diagnosis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model was ranked first in the China Physiological Signal Challenge 2018 competition.
alignments.rationale: Publication names the specific competition, clarifying the preprint's more generic statement while preserving the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model adequately predicted concurrent cardiac arrhythmias in patients diagnosed with multiple CA types.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Concurrent cardiac arrhythmias were adequately predictive for 476 patients with multiple types of diagnoses in the dataset.
alignments.rationale: Publication provides the same finding with an added concrete sample size (476), clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using single lead data resulted in only slightly worse performance compared to using full 12 lead data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using only single-lead data yielded a performance that was only slightly worse than using the full 12-lead data.
alignments.rationale: The wording is effectively identical and the reported relationship between single-lead and 12-lead performance is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis suggests the potential clinical application of certain single leads, such as aVR, for cardiac arrhythmia diagnosis.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not include any statement about clinical application or recommending specific leads (e.g., aVR), so this preprint claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Structural MRI data can significantly separate ADHD from control participants for both children and adults.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Structural MRI data can significantly separate ADHD from control participants for both children and adults.
alignments.rationale: The claim is verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Prediction performance and effect sizes were better for the child than the adult samples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Prediction performance and effect sizes were better for the child than the adult samples.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in both versions regarding relative performance and effect sizes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model trained on adult samples significantly predicted ADHD in the child sample.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model trained on adult samples significantly predicted ADHD in the child sample.
alignments.rationale: The predictive transfer result is stated the same in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These results support the continuity of ADHD’s pathophysiology from childhood to adulthood.
alignments.pub_claim_text: These results support the continuity of ADHD’s brain differences from childhood to adulthood.
alignments.rationale: Publication rephrases 'pathophysiology' as 'brain differences', a modest clarification in wording without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our work demonstrates a novel use of neural network classification models to test hypotheses about developmental continuity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our work demonstrates a novel use of neural network classification models to test hypotheses about developmental continuity.
alignments.rationale: Contribution statement remains the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study found a quasi-hierarchical functional organization in the human auditory cortex that qualitatively matched the hidden layers of deep neural networks trained in an automatic speech recognizer.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a quasi-hierarchical functional organization in the human auditory cortex that qualitatively matches the hidden layers of deep artificial neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The result is restated with equivalent meaning and same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Modifying the hidden layer organization of the artificial neural network based on neural activation patterns in human brains resulted in a substantial improvement in word recognition accuracy and learned speech representations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Modifying the hidden layer organization of an artificial neural network based on neural activation patterns in human brains leads to a substantial improvement in word recognition accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same causal improvement in recognition accuracy after modification based on neural data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrated that artificial and brain neural networks can be mutually informative in the domain of speech recognition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Artificial and brain neural networks can be mutually informative in the domain of speech recognition.
alignments.rationale: Conclusion is the same in both preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The microbial 'language' model captures interactions among different microbial taxa and common compositional patterns in microbial communities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The microbial 'language' model captures the interactions among different microbial taxa and the common compositional patterns in microbial communities.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is restated equivalently in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learned model produces contextualized taxa representations that allow a single microbial taxon to be represented differently according to its specific microbial environment.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pre-trained, context-sensitive embedding captures meaningful biological information, including taxonomic relationships and correlations with biological pathways.
alignments.rationale: Publication frames the contextualized taxa representations as context-sensitive embeddings that capture taxonomic and pathway-related biological information, a clarification of the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our sample representation consistently leads to improved performance for multiple prediction tasks, including predicting Irritable Bowel Disease (IBD) and diet patterns.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The sample representation produced by the model performs comparably to baseline models in predicting Irritable Bowel Disease (IBD) and diet patterns.
alignments.rationale: Preprint's claim of consistent improvement is weakened in the publication to 'comparable' performance versus baselines, representing a clear revision and tempering of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The IBD prediction model generalizes well to microbiome data independently collected from different populations with substantial distribution shift.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model significantly outperforms baseline models when generalizing to test data from independent studies, even in the presence of substantial distribution shifts.
alignments.rationale: Publication strengthens and clarifies the generalization claim by reporting significant outperformance of baselines on independent-study test data, broadening and amplifying the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model exhibits meaningful phylum-level structure despite never being exposed to such a signal.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pre-trained, context-sensitive embedding captures meaningful biological information, including taxonomic relationships and correlations with biological pathways.
alignments.rationale: Publication's description of embeddings capturing taxonomic relationships corresponds to the preprint's observation of emergent phylum-level structure, a clarification into biological interpretation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study introduces a novel method for identifying and mapping glioma-innervating neurons (GINs) using rabies virus-based monosynaptic tracing.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not explicitly state the introduction of a novel rabies-virus-based tracing method, so the methodological- novelty claim appears omitted from the highlighted publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Comprehensive whole-brain mapping revealed that GINs consistently arise from brain regions known to project to glioma locations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Comprehensive whole-brain mapping revealed that glioma-innervating neurons (GINs) originate from diverse neuromodulatory centers and specific cortical layers.
alignments.rationale: Publication refines the mapping result by specifying neuromodulatory centers and cortical layers; this clarifies the preprint's broader statement about origin regions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Long-range GINs are predominantly glutamatergic, while local striatal GINs are largely GABAergic, indicating a complex neuromodulatory profile.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Molecular profiling showed that long-range cortical GINs are predominantly glutamatergic, while local striatal GINs are largely GABAergic.
alignments.rationale: The molecular profiling result is the same in both versions, with slightly more precise wording in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GINs exhibit unique action potential characteristics that are distinct from similarly selected neurons in non-tumor-bearing brains.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Electrophysiological studies demonstrate that GINs have altered action potential waveforms compared to cortex-innervating neurons.
alignments.rationale: Publication provides a more specific comparator (cortex-innervating neurons) and frames the electrophysiological differences as altered waveforms, clarifying the preprint statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings indicate that GINs integrate into existing neural networks in a location-specific manner.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The observed intrinsic electrophysiological differences in GINs lay the groundwork for future investigations into their relationship with glioma cell characteristics.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's integrative-location-specific conclusion is softened in the publication to a more cautious statement about groundwork for future investigations relating GIN properties to glioma characteristics, representing a revision and tempering of the original interpretive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Local genomic sequences significantly impact the rate and fitness effect of CNVs and the rate of adaptation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The removal of local DNA elements significantly impacts the fitness effect of GAP1 CNVs and the rate of adaptation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same core finding but frames it around removal of local DNA elements and explicitly references GAP1 CNVs, which narrows and clarifies the original general claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Between 26% and 80% of all GAP1 CNVs are mediated by Origin Dependent Inverted Repeat Amplification (ODIRA). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Between 26% and 80% of all GAP1 CNVs are mediated by Origin Dependent Inverted Repeat Amplification (ODIRA) in 177 CNV lineages. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same quantitative claim but adds the sample context (177 CNV lineages), a clarifying detail without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Template switching during DNA replication is a frequent source of adaptive CNVs. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Template switching during DNA replication is a prevalent source of adaptive CNVs. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same conclusion using synonymous language (frequent/prevalent) with no substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In the absence of local ARS, a distal ARS can mediate CNV formation via ODIRA. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In the absence of local ARS, distal ones mediate CNV formation via ODIRA. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same mechanistic claim about distal ARS mediating CNV formation via ODIRA; wording and certainty are effectively unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Homologous recombination mechanisms can still mediate gene amplification in the absence of local LTRs. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In the absence of local LTRs, homologous recombination can mediate gene amplification following de novo retrotransposon events. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core claim but specifies it occurs following de novo retrotransposon events, which narrows and clarifies the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed ARA-CNN model achieves exceptional classification accuracy, outperforming other models trained on the same dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed ARA-CNN model achieves exceptional classification accuracy, outperforming other models trained on the same dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claim verbatim with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The network outputs an uncertainty measurement for each tested image. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The network outputs an uncertainty measurement for each tested image. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim retained in the publication without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Uncertainty measures can be used to detect mislabelled training samples and can be employed in an efficient active learning workflow. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Uncertainty measures can be used to detect mislabelled training samples and can be employed in an efficient active learning workflow. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this conclusion exactly; no change in meaning, scope, or expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using a variational dropout-based entropy measure of uncertainty in the workflow speeds up the learning process by roughly 45%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using a variational dropout-based entropy measure of uncertainty in the workflow speeds up the learning process by roughly 45%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Exact numeric performance improvement claim is preserved in the publication with no substantive changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can segment whole-slide images of colorectal tissue and compute segmentation-based spatial statistics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model can segment whole-slide images of colorectal tissue and compute segmentation-based spatial statistics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this application claim verbatim; no changes detected.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Biological noise plays a crucial role in gene regulation mechanisms. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Biological noise plays a crucial role in gene regulation mechanisms. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The high-level conclusion about biological noise is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed deep learning algorithm can predict the conditional probability distribution of a phenotype of interest with a small number of observations per input condition. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed deep learning algorithm can predict the conditional probability distribution of a phenotype of interest with a small number of observations per input condition. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this methodological/result claim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep neural network automatically generates probability distributions based on 10 or fewer noisy measurements for each input condition, without prior knowledge of the distributions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep neural network automatically generates the probability distributions based on 10 or less noisy measurements for each input condition. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the specific low-sample capability (10 or fewer) with essentially identical meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach is useful for exploring unknown biological systems with limited measurements, enhancing quantitative understanding and aiding in the design of new biological systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate this broader usefulness/impact claim; instead it adds a different claim about limitations of traditional models, so the original conclusion is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep learning model, GraTransDRP, improves drug representation and reduces information redundancy. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep learning model, GraTransDRP, improves drug representation and reduces information redundancy. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The core contribution claim is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Graph transformer is utilized in GraTransDRP to extract drug representation more efficiently. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Graph transformer is utilized in GraTransDRP to extract drug representation more efficiently. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Methodological/result claim retained verbatim with same confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that GraTransDRP outperforms state-of-the-art methods, including GraphDRP and GraOmicDRP. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that GraTransDRP outperforms state-of-the-art methods, including GraphDRP and GraOmicDRP. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is identical in the publication, preserving scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: KernelPCA is applied to transcriptomics features to reduce their dimension before they are processed by the CNN model. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: KernelPCA is applied to transcriptomics features to reduce their dimension before they are processed by the CNN model. (certainty=high, type=method)
alignments.rationale: The procedure is the same, but the publication reports it with stronger certainty and frames it as a formal method, indicating amplified confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We have developed a dimension reduction method that combines artificial intelligence with our knowledge of in vivo mutation association mechanisms. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed dimension reduction method combines artificial intelligence with knowledge of in vivo mutation association mechanisms to process large genomic data efficiently. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core contribution but adds an explicit application (processing large genomic data efficiently), clarifying and broadening the stated purpose.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our pipeline allows the generation of a simulated population meeting several essential criteria. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pipeline developed can generate a simulated population of genomic data that is diverse and realistic while adhering to linkage disequilibrium patterns found in humans. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the 'essential criteria' (diversity, realism, LD adherence), which clarifies and constrains the original general claim about the pipeline's outputs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It demonstrates good diversity, closely resembling that found in the reference dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pipeline developed can generate a simulated population of genomic data that is diverse and realistic while adhering to linkage disequilibrium patterns found in humans. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly states the diversity and realism claim; this is a direct clarification of the preprint's result about resemblance to reference data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is plausible, as newly generated combinations of mutations do not disrupt the linkage disequilibria found in humans. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pipeline developed can generate a simulated population of genomic data that is diverse and realistic while adhering to linkage disequilibrium patterns found in humans. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication converts the preprint's plausibility conclusion into a stronger, explicit result about adherence to LD patterns and reports it with higher certainty, so the claim is clarified and its confidence amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It also preserves donor anonymity by synthesising combinations of reference genomes that are distant from reference samples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The synthetic genomes produced by the pipeline do not reveal the genetic identity of any individual donor and differ from reference samples. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the anonymity claim in equivalent terms (does not reveal identity and differs from references), preserving meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Allo, a new approach for allocating multi-mapped reads, demonstrates enhanced accuracy in multi-mapping read assignment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Allo, a new approach for allocating multimapped reads, enhances accuracy in multimapping read assignment. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The wording and claim are effectively identical (new method improves multimapped read assignment), so the claim is unchanged in meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using Allo on CTCF ChIP-seq data results in the discovery of thousands of new CTCF peaks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using Allo on CTCF ChIP-seq data results in the discovery of thousands of new CTCF peaks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim and emphasis in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Allo identifies multiple previously unidentified interactions between transcription factors and repetitive element families when applied to ENCODE ChIP-seq datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Allo identifies multiple previously unidentified interactions between transcription factors and repetitive element families. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same result reported; publication omits the ENCODE dataset mention but the core claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Allo may be particularly effective in identifying ChIP-seq peaks in younger transposable elements, which hold evolutionary significance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Allo may be particularly beneficial in identifying ChIP-seq peaks at centromeres and near segmentally duplicated genes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both are tentative conclusions about where Allo is effective, but the publication changes the specific genomic targets (younger transposable elements -> centromeres/segmental duplications), so the claim content is revised and more narrowly focused.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Drosophila larvae process cold stimuli through functionally diverse somatosensory circuitry responsible for generating stimulus-specific behaviors. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Drosophila larvae process cold stimuli through functionally diverse somatosensory circuitry responsible for generating stimulus-specific behaviors. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The high-level conclusion is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural silencing of cell types downstream of CIII md neurons led to significant reductions in cold-evoked behaviors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural silencing of cell types downstream of CIII md neurons led to significant reductions in cold-evoked behaviors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same experimental result reported without meaningful change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Optogenetic activation of CIII md neurons evokes calcium increases in these neurons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Optogenetic activation of CIII md neurons evokes calcium increases in these neurons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Direct match between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multisensory integrators and specific neuron types are required for cold-evoked behavioral and calcium responses. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multisensory integrators, premotor, and projection neurons are required for cold-evoked behavioral and calcium responses. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the neuronal classes (premotor and projection neurons) implied by the preprint's "specific neuron types," so the claim is clarified and somewhat narrowed to named cell types.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Female AD mice exhibited anxiety-like behavior and cognitive decline at an earlier age than control mice and male mice. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Female APP/PS1 mice exhibited anxiety-like behavior and cognitive decline at an earlier age than control mice and male mice. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the AD model (APP/PS1), narrowing the original broader reference to "AD mice," but the substantive finding is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Brain-wide analysis of c-Fos+ revealed changes in regional correlations and overall network connectivity in AD mice. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Brainwide analysis of c-Fos+ revealed changes in regional correlations and overall network connectivity in APP/PS1 mice. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core result is the same; the publication clarifies the mouse model (APP/PS1) rather than generic "AD mice."

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In the ADNI dataset, anxiety predicted transition to dementia. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Anxiety predicted transition to dementia in the Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative dataset. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same observational result and dataset across preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Female subjects positive for anxiety and amyloid transitioned more quickly to dementia than male subjects. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Female participants positive for anxiety and amyloid transitioned more quickly to dementia than male participants. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Equivalent claim with minor wording differences (subjects -> participants).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AD network dysfunction is sexually dimorphic, suggesting that personalized medicine may benefit male and female AD patients differently. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are sex differences in AD network dysfunction, suggesting that personalized medicine may benefit male and female patients with AD. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is the same, but the publication reports lower certainty (medium -> low), so the confidence is tempered though the semantic content remains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The lexical semantic content of a sentence is primarily responsible for the ANN-to-brain similarity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The lexical-semantic content of the sentence is primarily responsible for the ANN-to-brain similarity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is restated with equivalent meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Perturbation manipulations that adversely affect brain predictivity also lead to more divergent representations in the ANN's embedding space. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Perturbation manipulations that adversely affect brain predictivity also lead to more divergent representations in the ANN's embedding space. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Direct match of experimental result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results are robust regardless of whether the mapping model is trained on intact or perturbed stimuli. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results are robust regardless of whether the mapping model is trained on intact or perturbed stimuli. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same methodological robustness claim in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work highlights the strength of systematic experimental manipulations for evaluating models of the human language network. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work highlights the strength of systematic experimental manipulations for evaluating models of the human language network. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Identical contribution statement.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The critical result aligns with the idea that the goal of the human language system is to extract meaning from linguistic strings. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes an additional interpretive conclusion (linking results to the goal of human language system) that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BEATRICE achieves uniformly better coverage with comparable power and set sizes compared to two state-of-the-art baseline methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BEATRICE achieves uniformly better coverage with comparable power and set sizes compared to two state-of-the-art baseline methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Exact match of performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance gain of BEATRICE increases with the number of causal variants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance gain of BEATRICE increases with the number of causal variants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same reported relationship between performance and number of causal variants.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BEATRICE successfully finds the APOE ε2 allele, a commonly associated variant of Alzheimer's disease, which the baseline methods could not identify. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Only BEATRICE can successfully find the APOE ϵ2 allele, a commonly associated variant of Alzheimer's. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims concern APOE ε2 detection; the publication states exclusivity ("only BEATRICE"), strengthening the preprint's claim that baselines could not identify it, so the claim is clarified and confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BEATRICE is a valuable tool to identify causal variants from eQTL and GWAS summary statistics across complex diseases and traits. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BEATRICE is effective in finding causal variants from the GWAS study of Alzheimer's disease. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint made a broad claim about eQTL/GWAS across complex traits; the publication presents a narrower, concrete application to an Alzheimer's GWAS, so the claim is revised and narrowed in scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neurons with similar responses are preferentially connected both within and across layers and areas, suggesting the universality of the 'like-to-like' connectivity across the visual hierarchy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neurons with similar response properties are preferentially connected within and across layers and areas, supporting the universality of 'like-to-like' connectivity across the visual hierarchy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with equivalent scope and confidence, preserving the original claim content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Only the feature component of neuronal tuning predicts fine-scale synaptic connections, beyond what could be explained by the physical proximity of axons and dendrites. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Only the feature component of neuronal tuning predicts fine-scale synaptic connections beyond what could be explained by the proximity of axons and dendrites. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same specific finding about feature tuning predicting fine-scale connectivity with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Disrupting 'like-to-like' connections has a significantly greater impact on performance compared to lesions of random connections in recurrent neural networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Disrupting like-to-like connections in recurrent neural networks impairs performance more than disrupting random connections. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same experimental result about targeted disruption versus random lesions in recurrent networks without changing the claim's meaning or strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These connectivity principles may play a functional role in sensory processing and learning, highlighting shared principles between biological and artificial systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These connectivity principles may have a functional role in sensory processing and learning, highlighting shared principles between biological and artificial systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion about functional roles and shared principles with similar certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Postsynaptic neuron cohorts downstream of presynaptic cells show greater functional similarity than predicted by a pairwise like-to-like rule. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This result appears in the publication but has no counterpart in the preprint; it is a novel observation about cohort-level similarity beyond pairwise rules.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed method achieves an accuracy of 0.91 for the test set, outperforming existing GNN-based methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed method achieves an accuracy of 0.91 for the test set, outperforming existing GNN-based methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical performance claim and comparison are reported in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach for validating protein-protein interfaces uses learned interface representations through a graph-based contrastive autoencoder architecture and a transformer. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach for validating protein-protein interfaces uses learned interface representations through a graph-based contrastive autoencoder architecture and a transformer. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same methodological description without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method provides a promising solution for validating protein-protein interfaces. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method provides a promising solution for validating protein-protein interfaces. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding assessment is retained verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The NCSN model yields the most accurate transformations for MR images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Probabilistic algorithms are more accurate than other benchmark methods for MR image transformation, with diffusion models yielding the most accurate results. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint asserted NCSN as the single most accurate model, while the publication shifts to diffusion models overall being most accurate, changing which model is claimed best and broadening to a model class.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DDPM model yields variance results that most clearly detect out-of-distribution (OOD) input images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Within the diffusion models, DDPM demonstrates higher performance in terms of similarity metrics, while NCSN exhibits accurate distributional measures and computationally favorable characteristics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not support the specific preprint claim that DDPM variance most clearly detects OOD; instead it reports DDPM's strengths on similarity metrics, which changes and narrows the original OOD-specific claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The cGAN model is significantly more efficient in generating multiple samples compared to the two diffusion models (NCSN and DDPM). (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or support the preprint's claim about cGAN sample-generation efficiency, and instead emphasizes diffusion-model characteristics; the specific cGAN efficiency claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Probabilistic conditional generative models are useful for MR image transformation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Probabilistic conditional generative algorithms can be applied to MR image transformation and can quantify performance. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both documents assert that probabilistic conditional generative models are applicable and useful for MR image transformation with similar emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Generating an ensemble of outputs helps in identifying OOD input images. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ability to generate multiple transformed images for a given input image can be used to estimate uncertainty in the output and detect out-of-distribution input images. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly states the same utility of multiple outputs for uncertainty estimation and OOD detection.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Generative models can be used to assess confidence in a given transformation and detect input images that contain pathology and/or artifacts. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This publication conclusion about detecting pathology/artifacts using generative models extends the preprint's focus and appears as an additional published claim not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best model for each breast cancer cell subtype can achieve high predictive accuracy for the test sets with AUC values of 0.689–0.993. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best model for each breast cancer cell subtype can achieve high predictive accuracy for the test sets with AUC values of 0.689-0.993. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The predictive-performance claim is reproduced identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Important structural fragments related to breast cancer cell inhibition were identified and interpreted. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Important structural fragments related to breast cancer cell inhibition were identified and interpreted. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same result about identified structural fragments without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An online webserver called ChemBC and its local version software were developed to predict potential anti-breast cancer agents. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An online webserver called ChemBC was developed to predict whether compounds have potential inhibitory activity against breast cancer cells. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the webserver contribution but omits mention of a local software version, narrowing the original claim to the online resource only.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental evidence suggests that for visual tasks, intermediate results are stored in working memory as an enhancement of neural activity in the visual cortex. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental evidence suggests that intermediate results of the elemental operations are stored in working memory as an enhancement of neural activity in the visual cortex. (certainty=low, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same idea but with lower reported certainty and specifies 'elemental operations,' weakening the strength of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a new recurrent architecture capable of solving composite visual tasks in a reinforcement learning context. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a new recurrent neural network architecture that can learn composite visual tasks that require the application of successive elemental operations. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same architectural contribution, with consistent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The networks learned an abstract, general rule and selected the appropriate sequence of elemental operations, solely based on the characteristics of the visual stimuli and the reward structure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Networks learn elemental operations, such as contour grouping and visual search, and execute sequences of operations based on the characteristics of the visual stimuli and the reward structure of a task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both texts report that networks learn and sequence elemental operations driven by stimuli and reward; the publication provides examples but does not change the core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The activity of the units of the neural network resembled the activity of neurons in the visual cortex of monkeys solving the same tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: After training was completed, the activity of the units of the neural network elicited by behaviorally relevant image items was stronger than that elicited by irrelevant ones. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasized resemblance to monkey neural activity; the publication instead reports strengthened responses to relevant items, which reframes and narrows the original comparison to biological neurons.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results demonstrate how a biologically plausible learning rule can train a recurrent neural network on multistep visual tasks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our results demonstrate how a biologically plausible learning rule can train a recurrent neural network on multistep visual tasks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim about training RNNs with a biologically plausible rule is preserved verbatim.
